Title: Data Scientist
Description:
COSMOTE Global Solutions, part of the OTE Group of Companies, is seeking a skilled Data Scientist to join our dynamic team. The ideal candidate will leverage data analysis, machine learning, and statistical techniques to extract valuable insights that drive informed business decisions and innovation.

Key Responsibilities:


Analyze large and complex datasets to identify trends, patterns, and insights
Develop, test, and implement machine learning models and algorithms
Collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand business challenges and provide data-driven solutions
Create data visualizations and reports to communicate findings effectively to stakeholders
Ensure data quality and integrity throughout the analysis process
Continuously research and apply new data science techniques and technologies


Requirements


Bachelor's degree or higher in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field
Minimum 13 years of previous relavant experience in IT
Strong proficiency in programming languages such as Python or R
Experience with machine learning frameworks and libraries (e.g., TensorFlow, scikit-learn)
Hands-on experience with data visualization tools (e.g., Tableau, Power BI)
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills
Familiarity with cloud platforms such as Azure or AWS is a plus
Excellent communication skills in English, both written and verbal

Title: Data Science Internship
HTML:
Job Location

Brussels

Job Description

Do you have passion for technology and desire to learn and grow continuously? Do you want to belong to one of the most advanced Data Science & Analytics group in the industry, developing sophisticated mathematical models and algorithms and applying Operations Research techniques?

Then join P&G as a Data Science intern for Supply Chain! Through your internship, you will build your Data Science and Analytics skills, develop your ability to influence and think strategically and learn how to work across domains and functions to make P&G’s supply chain more performant, more resilient and more sustainable.

Find out more about IT at P&G at www.youtube.com/watch?v=1ial3aeA9SE.

AS A DATA SCIENCE FOR SUPPLY CHAIN INTERN, YOU WILL:

  • Influence decisions from daily through to strategic ones, made across functions, at all levels of leadership, to have a meaningful impact on the performance, resiliency and environmental footprint of one of the largest CPG companies in the world;
  • Build the next generation of supply chain analytics, using:
    • Mathematical modeling techniques: decision analysis, optimization, machine learning, simulation etc.;
    • Programming languages (at least one of these): Python, SQL, R, Hive/Impala, MATLAB, etc.;
    • Data Visualization: Power BI, Tableau, etc.
    • Work with technology partners to translate your innovations into robust, scaled, analytical solutions;
    • Communicate insights from complex data or algorithms into simple conclusions that will empower others to act based on the insights you derive;
    • Belong to an award-winning Analytics / Operations Research community that is global in scope and operates across P&G’s supply chains.

    Job Qualifications

    YOU ARE THE RIGHT FIT IF YOU:

    • Are currently pursuing a Master's degree in a quantitative field (Engineering, Statistics, Operation Research, Decisions Sciences, Computer Science, Applied Math, Economics, Data Science, etc.);
    • Have strong analytical skills and proficiency in at least one programming language (R, Python, SQL, etc.) or family of techniques (optimization, simulation, machine learning, predictive modeling, etc.);
    • Are adaptable and able to handle multiple priorities effectively, both independently and as part of a collaborative team;
    • Are proficient in English, both written and spoken;

    We are offering internships of at least 3 months throughout the year, as of May 2026.

    If studying at a Belgian university, please make sure you consult with the internship department at your school to agree on the paid internship we offer.

    Your Advantages

    • A temporary contract for the duration of your internship, with an attractive compensation package;
    • Monthly allowance: 2366 EUR brut/month, prorated 13th month based on the months worked during your internship period;
    • A flexible benefits plan to customize to your personal needs and kilometer allowance/public transport reduction;
    • Access to Benefits at work platform: A large benefits platform with employee discounts on renowned shops and online commercial platforms as well as on leisure activities and well-known amusement parks;
    • Working in an international and inclusive environment, with diverse and supporting colleagues;
    • Continuous self-development via coaching, trainings and a general Growth Mindset working environment;
    • A vibrant and modern working environment, supporting employees’ wellbeing:
    • Subsidized company restaurant;
    • On-site vibrant Café for breaks and less formal meetings;
    • On-site fitness room and possibility to join regular group sport activities;
    • Flexibility in terms of working hours;
    • Employee Assistance Program: free professional counseling services, work-life resources, and mental health support.
    • Supporting employee's commute: plenty of parking facilities for both cars and bikes, shower facilities, shuttle between Heizel metro and the office, carpooling community.

    ARE YOU EXCITED ABOUT THE ROLE? DEEP DIVE INTO P&G AND CHECK THE BELOW INSIGHTFUL LINKS:

    • Our hiring process consists of three main steps: 1) application, 2) online assessments and 3) interviews. Click here to get all the tips to be fully prepared.
    • P&G is driven to make life better, not just within the company, but across the globe. Click here to check out how we make a meaningful impact on the world.
    • We are the world’s largest consumer goods company. Click here to discover about our iconic brands.

    At P&G #weseeequal

    We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. At P&G we strive to build a culture where everyone feels welcome, included, and able to bring their full selves to work.

    We ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process. Please click here if you require an accommodation during the application process. Please make sure to wait to hear back from us regarding your accommodation before proceeding with the online assessment, we thank you in advance for your patience.

    Job Schedule

    Full time

    Job Number

    R000145698

    Job Segmentation

    Internships

    Starting Pay / Salary Range

    €2.366,00 / month

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
About SailPeak

At Sailpeak, we are a Brussels-based consulting firm specializing in guiding Banking & Insurance firms through the complexities of digital transformation.

With expertise in Business Strategy, Data & AI, and Digital Transformation, Sailpeak helps clients navigate the ever-evolving landscape and reach new heights of success.

🤖 About The Role

We are looking for a Data Scientist with 1-3 years of experience, working on AI-related projects to join our growing team.

In this role, you will drive digital strategy for top-tier banking clients by delivering high-impact projects focused on data analysis, performance optimization, and dashboard creation. You will play a pivotal part in expanding our internal AI capabilities, gaining hands-on experience with cutting-edge machine learning models to build innovative solutions for the financial services sector.

📊 You will:


Assist in data collection, cleansing, and transformation from diverse banking and fintech data sources, ensuring high-quality inputs for advanced analytics.
Contribute to Machine Learning and Deep Learning projects, gaining exposure to AI models, intelligent agents, and digital assistants applied within the financial services sector.
Support the development of data-driven digital strategies and AI-powered campaigns tailored to banking clients.
Conduct A/B testing, hypothesis validation, and performance analysis of digital and AI-driven initiatives in the banking domain.
Create and maintain interactive dashboards, visualizations, and reports to monitor key metrics for digital banking strategies and AI adoption.
Collaborate with cross-functional and agile teams to optimize campaign segmentation, targeting, and personalization, leveraging predictive analytics.
Participate in data interpretation and storytelling, contributing to actionable recommendations that support banking digital transformation and AI initiatives.


📊 You bring to the table:


1-3 years of experience as a Data Scientist
A Master’s degree in Data Science, Statistics, Banking, Digital Transformation, or a related field.
A strong eagerness to work with data, AI, and machine learning models, and to grow expertise in digital transformation within banking.
Basic knowledge of data analysis and visualization tools such as Python, SQL, Power BI, Tableau, or VBA.
Familiarity with digital banking platforms, fintech ecosystems, and emerging AI technologies is a plus.
An interest in AI, intelligent agents, and digital assistants, and how they reshape customer journeys and operational efficiency in financial services.
Strong communication and data storytelling skills to translate insights into value for digital strategy and AI adoption.


🗣️ Language Skills

Fluency in English and French/Dutch

🤹 Other Skills


Experience with Scrum or Agile methods is a great plus for us.
We are looking for proactive problem-solvers
You’ll be working independently & autonomously
Passion for growing, building, and continuous learning, especially around the developments within AI and Data Science


🎁 What’s In It For You

💸 A competitive salary and benefits package.

🦾 Working on cutting-edge projects with leading banking clients, in a startup environment

💿 Exposure to a wide array of AI and data science technologies.

🫂 Mentorship from Directors and Partners with backgrounds from renowned companies like Google, PayPal, Semetis, and Sia Partners.

🧑‍🔬 The Process


Step 1: 30-minute call with our Head of Talent (Peter): focusing on your current situation, motivation, experience, and practicalities
Step 2: 45-minute deep dive into the projects you’ve worked on with one of our experienced consultants
Step 3: 30-minute call with one of the founders, talking in more depth about motivations and ambitions
Step 4: Onsite Business Case in our office:)
The Offer


Are you ready to embark on a rewarding career in management consulting with Sailpeak?

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
The Role

We’re partnering with an innovative ClimateTech company building intelligent AI-driven solutions that improve environmental sustainability, operational efficiency, and resource optimization. They are looking for a Machine Learning Engineer to develop and deploy scalable ML solutions for real-world environmental and industrial challenges.




What You’ll Be Doing

Develop and deploy machine learning models for sustainability-focused applications such as energy optimization, predictive maintenance, emissions tracking, anomaly detection, and environmental analytics
Create scalable ML infrastructure and pipelines for training, evaluation, monitoring, and real-time model serving across industrial and climate-focused platforms
Design data preprocessing and feature engineering workflows for complex datasets including IoT, sensor, geospatial, and time-series data
Help build intelligent monitoring and optimization systems leveraging technologies such as edge devices, remote sensing, connected infrastructure, and live sensor networks
Contribute to data-driven solutions that improve operational efficiency, reduce environmental impact, and support sustainability initiatives




What They’re Looking For

2–6+ years of experience in Machine Learning Engineering or similar AI-focused roles
Strong proficiency in Python
Hands-on experience with machine learning frameworks such as PyTorch, TensorFlow, or Scikit-learn
Track record of building, deploying, and maintaining ML systems in production
Experience working with environmental, industrial, energy, IoT, mobility, or geospatial datasets is considered a strong plus
Understanding of data engineering practices including ETL/ELT pipelines, streaming systems, and large-scale time-series data processing
Experience with cloud infrastructure such as AWS, Azure, or GCP
Interest in applying AI to sustainability, clean technology, smart infrastructure, or environmental intelligence challenges




What’s on Offer

Hybrid working model with offices located in Aalst
Opportunity to contribute to cutting-edge ClimateTech solutions with tangible environmental impact
Collaborative environment with a strong emphasis on innovation, experimentation, and applied AI
Competitive salary package up to €6,000/month depending on experience
Company car or flexible mobility budget
13+ additional ADV leave days
Clear career progression opportunities toward senior and leadership roles
Mission-driven culture focused on building technology for a more sustainable future




If this opportunity excites you, apply today or send your CV and a short cover letter to ryan.martin@vividresourcing.com.

Title: Data Scientist - Consulting & AI Delivery
Description:
Are you excited by solving real business problems with data, analytics, and AI?

Do you enjoy combining structured analysis, client interaction, and modern tools to create measurable impact?

Are you curious about new ways of working, including AI-assisted and agentic workflows, while staying pragmatic and quality-driven?

At Agilytic, we help clients turn business objectives into measurable results through smarter use of data. We are looking for a colleague who is comfortable moving between business understanding, quantitative analysis, and hands-on delivery.

What is your mission?

Help innovative companies turn their objectives into concrete results through smarter use of data, analytics, automation, and AI.

You will work at the intersection of business and technology to help clients make better decisions, improve performance, and implement solutions that create tangible value.

What will you do day to day?

As a Data Scientist (Consulting & AI Delivery), you will contribute to the end-to-end delivery of our projects, from problem framing to implementation and client adoption.

You will work closely with client stakeholders and internal team members to:


Translate business questions into analytical approaches and decision-support solutions
Structure and prioritize requirements with clients
Collect, clean, transform, and validate data from multiple sources
Perform descriptive, diagnostic, and predictive analyses where relevant
Build dashboards, models, reports, and decision-support tools
Prototype and automate parts of the analysis or delivery workflow
Communicate findings, trade-offs, and recommendations clearly to technical and non-technical audiences
Support implementation so that analysis leads to concrete action
Use modern AI-assisted tools responsibly to accelerate research, coding, testing, documentation, and delivery


Our assignments typically include:


Data audit
Data preparation
Descriptive and diagnostic analysis
Modeling where relevant
Dashboarding and reporting
Presenting results and recommendations
Supporting implementation and business adoption


Senior Data Scientists also supervise and coach junior team members in both client delivery and internal initiatives. This guidance helps them quickly develop the business, consulting, and technical skills needed to manage projects independently.

How We Work

We are pragmatic and outcome-driven. We use the right level of sophistication for the problem at hand.

That means we do not apply advanced techniques for their own sake. Sometimes the right answer is a predictive model, an automation, or a more advanced analytical workflow. Sometimes it is a clear dashboard, a robust dataset, or a simple analysis that helps a client act quickly and confidently.

Increasingly, this also includes AI-assisted and agentic ways of working. We welcome candidates who are already comfortable using tools such as Claude Code, Codex, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, or similar tools to accelerate research, coding, testing, documentation, and delivery.

What matters most is not tool usage alone, but professional judgment: being able to frame the problem correctly, validate outputs carefully, communicate clearly, and maintain quality, security, and maintainability.

Requirements

Do you recognize yourself?

Your background


You hold a master's degree in a quantitative, scientific, engineering, or business-related field such as Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, Physics, Biology, Computer Science, or similar
You are fluent in English and in either Dutch or French


Your core skills


You have a strong foundation in analytics, statistics, and structured problem solving
You have hands-on experience with Python and SQL for data preparation, analysis, and modeling
You are comfortable working with real-world datasets and transforming ambiguous business questions into structured analytical work
You can communicate insights clearly and adapt your message to both technical and non-technical audiences
You are comfortable working directly with clients and presenting recommendations


Nice to have


Experience with ETL / ELT workflows, cloud data environments, APIs, or analytics engineering
Experience with BI and reporting tools
Familiarity with machine learning, forecasting, segmentation, optimization, or experimentation
Experience with version control, testing, and collaborative development practices
Familiarity with AI-assisted or agentic coding workflows and tools such as Claude Code, Codex, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, or similar


We will be especially interested in candidates who can show how they use modern tools to work faster and better while maintaining strong standards of rigor, validation, and documentation.

Your personal style


You have strong analytical skills and can collect, structure, analyze, and interpret data in service of a clear business objective
You have a problem-solving mindset and take a pragmatic approach to data science
You look for the simplest solution that creates real value
You are proactive, autonomous, and comfortable taking initiative
You care about work quality and bring a professional attitude to client service
You are curious about new techniques and tools and keep learning
You combine business understanding with enough technical depth to deliver credible, useful work


Must have

You are eligible to work in Belgium and the European Union Please note that we do not offer relocation support

You will be a great fit if


You want to help shape a growing organization and grow with it
You enjoy combining business understanding with hands-on analytical work
You can move from ambiguity to structured action
You are comfortable interacting with stakeholders at all levels, from operational teams to executive leadership
You are curious about better ways of working, including AI-assisted delivery
You care about clarity, quality, and measurable client impact


Some signs this may not be the right fit


You do not enjoy interacting with clients or presenting your work from time to time All our Data Scientists are client-facing
You are not comfortable working at the client's premises on average 2 to 3 days a week and from our offices in Brussels on average 1 to 2 days a week
You are looking for a purely technical role with little business interaction
You are not comfortable working in a pragmatic consulting environment where the best solution is not always the most technically sophisticated one
You do not have an entrepreneurial mindset and are not motivated to go the extra mile to deliver quality work or contribute to the growth of the organization


Benefits

Working at Agilytic

We aim to create a work environment that allows you to do exceptional work, grow your skills, and enjoy the journey while doing it.

You may work on assignments anywhere in Belgium and neighboring countries such as Luxembourg, France, the Netherlands, and the UK. You can expect to interact with both IT/data specialists and business decision-makers across areas such as marketing, process improvement, and customer care.

We believe that data is a means to an end, not an end in itself. We do not use advanced techniques when they are not suited to the problem we need to solve. For example, we will not build a complex machine learning model if a well-structured dashboard or a focused analysis answers the client's question more effectively. In practice, a significant share of our missions do not require machine learning at all.

This is a role for someone who wants to combine analytical depth, business relevance, and modern ways of working in a consulting environment.

Salary And Benefits


Competitive remuneration package: We offer a competitive remuneration package, including a cafeteria plan in which you can allocate your variable remuneration to the advantages that suit you best, such as extra days off, electronics, or cash
Training roadmap: We will define a training roadmap together, taking into account both our internal capabilities and your personal interests
Supportive structure and transparency: What we do not yet have in scale, we make up for with flexibility, support, and transparency. You can expect individual coaching and a clear approach to career development
Modern tools: Tools are a means to an end. We want them to help you do your best work, not slow you down. You can expect up-to-date hardware and software, including modern AI-assisted tools where useful
A serious job that does not take itself too seriously:
We care deeply about the quality of our work, but we also like working with people who can keep things human


Application

If this sounds like the kind of role in which you would thrive, we would like to hear from you.

In your application, feel free to share examples of analyses, dashboards, code, automations, or AI-assisted workflows you have built. We are particularly interested in how you think, how you validate your work, and how you create business value.

  • Competitive remuneration package: We offer a competitive remuneration package, including a cafeteria plan in which you can allocate your variable remuneration to the advantages that suit you best, such as extra days off, electronics, or cash
  • Training roadmap: We will define a training roadmap together, taking into account both our internal capabilities and your personal interests
  • Supportive structure and transparency: What we do not yet have in scale, we make up for with flexibility, support, and transparency. You can expect individual coaching and a clear approach to career development
  • Modern tools: Tools are a means to an end. We want them to help you do your best work, not slow you down. You can expect up-to-date hardware and software, including modern AI-assisted tools where useful
  • A serious job that does not take itself too seriously:
  • We care deeply about the quality of our work, but we also like working with people who can keep things human

Application

If this sounds like the kind of role in which you would thrive, we would like to hear from you.

In your application, feel free to share examples of analyses, dashboards, code, automations, or AI-assisted workflows you have built. We are particularly interested in how you think, how you validate your work, and how you create business value.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist (ML & GenAI)- Remote Contract | Spain, Portugal or Sweden




We're hiring a Data Scientist with a strong ML background and a genuine interest in GenAI to join a global engineering team working on two really interesting internal products.




This is a mid-level role with a July start, running through to September with a high likelihood of extension beyond that. You'll be embedded across both projects, working alongside existing data scientists and contributing to real, production-grade solutions — not just keeping things at PoC stage.




The Projects




The first is a field service scheduling platform — a live, actively developed tool that manages when engineers need to go on-site for maintenance. There's a working live schedule already in place and the team are continuing to build and iterate on it.




The second is a GenAI-powered chatbot that's moving in a more ambitious direction — the team are rebuilding the UI and evolving the product significantly. It's earlier stage, which means more room to shape the direction, with some genuinely interesting GenAI concepts in play.




What You'll Be Doing




Supporting both projects as an embedded Data Scientist, working closely with the existing science team
Developing and deploying ML models including predictive, prescriptive, and generative AI solutions
Integrating LLMs and GenAI capabilities into analytical workflows and production systems
Building and maintaining ETL pipelines on AWS
Analysing complex datasets to surface patterns and insights that drive real decisions
Designing experiments, performing statistical analysis, and validating model performance
Collaborating with cross-functional teams to translate business requirements into working data science solutions
Building with production in mind — this team needs someone who can deploy and manage systems, not just experiment




What We're Looking For




Strong ML and data science fundamentals — statistical modelling, supervised/unsupervised learning, feature engineering, hypothesis testing
Hands-on GenAI experience — LLMs, prompt engineering, RAG concepts
Solid Python and SQL skills — these are non-negotiable
ETL pipeline experience, ideally on AWS
Some software engineering background — you're comfortable deploying things and managing systems in a production environment
Experience with deep learning frameworks such as TensorFlow or PyTorch
Familiarity with MLOps best practices, Docker, Git, and Jupyter
Strong problem-solving skills and the ability to work with less day-to-day direction
Based in Spain, Portugal, or Sweden




Nice to Have




Experience with data visualisation tools — matplotlib, seaborn, plotly
Knowledge of knowledge graphs and data connectivity
Familiarity with AWS CDK or similar IaC tooling
Experience working within large enterprise or industrial environments




The Details




Fully remote — B2B contract
Start date: beginning of July
Initial contract through to September — high chance of extension
Based in Spain, Portugal, or Sweden

Title: Data Scientist - Agriculture & Nature Analytics
Description:
Who You'll Work With

Driving lasting impact and building long-term capabilities with our clients is not easy work. You are the kind of person who thrives in a high performance/high reward culture - doing hard things, picking yourself up when you stumble, and having the resilience to try another way forward.

In return for your drive, determination, and curiosity, we'll provide the resources, mentorship, and opportunities you need to become a stronger leader faster than you ever thought possible. Your colleagues—at all levels—will invest deeply in your development, just as much as they invest in delivering exceptional results for clients. Every day, you'll receive apprenticeship, coaching, and exposure that will accelerate your growth in ways you won’t find anywhere else.

When you join us, you will have:


Continuous learning: Our learning and apprenticeship culture, backed by structured programs, is all about helping you grow while creating an environment where feedback is clear, actionable, and focused on your development. The real magic happens when you take the input from others to heart and embrace the fast-paced learning experience, owning your journey.
A voice that matters: From day one, we value your ideas and contributions. You’ll make a tangible impact by offering innovative ideas and practical solutions, all while upholding our unwavering commitment to ethics and integrity. We not only encourage diverse perspectives, but they are critical in driving us toward the best possible outcomes.
Global community: With colleagues across 65+ countries and over 100 different nationalities, our firm’s diversity fuels creativity and helps us come up with the best solutions for our clients. Plus, you’ll have the opportunity to learn from exceptional colleagues with diverse backgrounds and experiences.
World-class benefits: On top of a competitive salary (based on your location, experience, and skills), we provide a comprehensive benefits package to enable holistic well-being for you and your family.


Your Impact

You’ll work with McKinsey’s agricultural and nature analytics team (ACRE) in Brussels, Belgium, part of our Agriculture Practice and Nature & Natural Capital Service Line.

Our Agriculture Practice advises agribusiness, consumer food, government, and investor clients on strategic, marketing & sales, and operations issues, helping support industry-shaping decisions that impact the future of global food production.

The Nature & Natural Capital Service Line helps corporations, public-sector actors and institutions in enabling nature-positive outcomes by integrating biodiversity, land use, water, and ecosystem information into strategy, investment decisions, and operations.

Within this group, ACRE is a team of ~30 expert consultants, data scientists, and engineers focused on bringing cutting-edge analytics to our clients. We apply real-time data and advanced analytics or AI – generally predictive or geospatial in nature – to solve the biggest problems currently facing global agriculture and nature.

You will leverage industry knowledge and analytical expertise to provide insights both to clients as part of client service teams and within the ACRE team by strengthening the core products and algorithms we build for clients. You can expect to split your time delivering impact at clients (either remotely or on site) and building up ACRE’s core analytical offerings.

In the agriculture sector, issues to solve could include determining the suitability of growing different types of crops across different geographies, using local agronomic data and satellite imagery to determine market potential for input products, or optimizing farm operations using yield forecasting. For the nature sector, you may help public-sector clients from low- and middle-income clients define their nature conservation strategy, supporting them through geospatial analysis and your domain knowledge.

Along the way, you will receive best-in-class training in structuring business problems and serving as a client adviser, develop tech-skills through (in-person) trainings curated by big-tech companies, and have opportunities to work closely with and learn from our senior agriculture and nature leaders and industry players that are shaping the future of these fields. You will get access to unparalleled career acceleration, with a huge amount of ownership and responsibility from the get-go in a collaborative, diverse, non-hierarchical environment. You will get the opportunity to travel to client sites and attend in-person trainings, locally and around the world. Lastly, you will be able to provide direct and measurable impact towards solving some of the largest problems facing the global food-system and natural ecosystems.

Your Qualifications and Skills


Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in a quantitative and/or environmental-related discipline, such as agriculture, agronomy, biology, ecology, geography, climate, or related
Strong experience with agronomy/crop science, animal sciences and farm-level production systems, AND/OR nature- and natural-capital–related topics, such as biodiversity, land use, water systems, ecosystem services, or nature-related risk and impact assessment, preferred
Geospatial and spatiotemporal analysis, remote sensing, image processing, preferred
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, paired with the ability to develop creative and efficient solutions
Clean, efficient coding abilities in Python ideally with experience parallel processing and using cloud computing resources
Ability to design and develop geospatial workflows and pipelines for data analysis and processing; ability to use version-control & collaboration tools
Strong multitasking and parallel development abilities
Strong interpersonal communication skills
Creative, naturally curious, and willing to take intellectual risks
Able to work under competing, quickly-changing priorities, manage expectations effectively and support the team under pressure
Self-management skills and ability to work as part of an Agile team preferred
Willingness to travel up to 50%

Title: Freelance Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Please submit your CV in English and indicate your level of English proficiency.

Mindrift connects specialists with project-based AI opportunities for leading tech companies, focused on testing, evaluating, and improving AI systems. Participation is project-based, not permanent employment.

What This Opportunity Involves

While each project involves unique tasks, contributors may:


Design original computational STEM problems that simulate real scientific workflows
Create problems that require Python programming to solve
Ensure problems are computationally intensive and cannot be solved manually within reasonable timeframes (days/weeks)
Develop problems requiring non-trivial reasoning chains and creative problem-solving approaches
Verify solutions using Python with standard libraries (Numpy, Pandas, Scipy, scikit-learn)
Document problem statements clearly and provide verified correct answers


What We Look For

This opportunity is a good fit for ML specialists with an experience in python open to part-time, non-permanent projects. Ideally, contributors will have:


5+ years of hands-on machine learning experience with proven business impact
Portfolio of completed projects and publications showcasing real-world problem-solving
Expert Python programming for data science (pandas, numpy, scipy, scikit-learn, statsmodels)
Expert statistical analysis and machine learning - deep understanding of algorithms, methods, and their practical applications
Expert with SQL and database operations for data manipulation and analysis
Experience with GenAI technologies (LLMs, RAG, prompt engineering, vector databases)
Understanding of MLOps practices and model deployment workflows
Knowledge of modern frameworks (TensorFlow, PyTorch, LangChain)
Strong written English (C1+)


How It Works

Apply → Pass qualification(s) → Join a project → Complete tasks → Get paid

Project time expectations

For this project, tasks are estimated to require around 10-20 hours per week during active phases, based on project requirements. This is an estimate, not a guaranteed workload, and applies only while the project is active.

Compensation

On this project, contributors can earn up to $58 per hour equivalent, depending on their level and pace of contribution.

Compensation varies across projects depending on scope, complexity, and required expertise. Please note that other projects on the platform may offer different earning levels based on their requirements.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Sia is a next-generation, global management consulting group. Founded in 1999, we were born digital. Today our strategy and management capabilities are augmented by data science, enhanced by creativity and driven by responsibility. We’re optimists for change and we help clients initiate, navigate and benefit from transformation. We believe optimism is a force multiplier, helping clients to mitigate downside and maximize opportunity. With expertise across a broad range of sectors and services, our 3,000 consultants serve clients worldwide from 48 locations in 19 countries. Our expertise delivers results. Our optimism transforms outcomes.





Job Description



What you will do

As an expert in the field of Machine Learning Engineering you will be responsible for the growth of our Data team, both professionally as well as in size. You will be in charge of shaping and executing the future of your expertise within Sia. You will work on client projects as well as on building our own Sia AI solutions, together with our Dutch team of Data Scientists, our French Data Engineering team, and other business units of Sia Global.



Your responsibilities include:



Leading an internal team towards the development of AI products;
Working in a multidisciplinary team on client projects as an expert on your subject;
Making architectural and design decisions for cloud environments;
Building production level ML/AI solutions with solid software engineering;
Guiding Data Scientists to scale up their models into production (parallelizing, optimizing, tuning, testing, and wrapping);
Giving trainings, leading internal meetings and organizing external events;
Participating in team meetings and workshops with clients;
Writing blog articles, presenting at conferences and sending out press releases on the subject that you lead.

Who you will work with

The Data Science team is young, diverse and growing (10+). You will join a team that will challenge you and invest in your professional development. We have a flat structure and rely on the entrepreneurial spirit of our members: initiative is highly encouraged. We are striving for well-being, diversity and knowledge-sharing inside the team, so that we can grow together.



Who you are



You are an established technical professional that wants to make the next step in their career, taking on more leadership responsibilities.
You have first-rate project management and time management skills.
You are a true problem solver, you turn complex issues into simple and impactful business solutions.
You are enthusiastic for applying Data Science/Engineering solutions to business problems.
You have excellent communication skills and can present complicated concepts in a synthetic way.
You have the ability to adapt rapidly and work on a range of different business areas within a challenging environment.
You have the ability to work collaboratively in a team environment.

In return, you can expect:



An international company that gives importance to diversity, inclusion, employee well-being and corporate social responsibilities actions.
A great working atmosphere with friendly and professional colleagues where innovation and entrepreneurship are encouraged
A personal development plan with a regular follow-up as well training opportunities to develop yourself
A competitive salary with great advantages, like mobility budget, hospital insurance, performance -related bonus,...


Qualifications


MSc level education in Computer Science, Applied Mathematics, Artificial Intelligence, Embedded Systems or related field of study.
2-4 years of experience in building production-quality ML/Deep Learning pipelines and models.
Good understanding of Machine Learning fundamentals and strong knowledge in a specific domain (e.g., Computer Vision, Deep Learning, NLP).
Expert coding skills in Python and at least one of these languages: Scala, C++, Java.
Hands-on experience with database management languages (e.g., SQL, PostgreSQL).
Hands-on experience in cloud-based infrastructures (AWS/GCP/Azure).
Hands-on experience with Unix-based command line and DevOps tools (Git, Docker, Kubernetes).
Hands-on experience with big data technologies (e.g. Spark, Hadoop, Databricks).
You have a strong interest in data and AI-related topics, the ability to quickly build expertise in these areas, and you’re excited about helping our clients seize the opportunities they offer.
Experience with coaching/mentoring other engineers.
Prior experience in Management Consulting is a strong plus.
Willingness to travel and work at local and international clients.
Fluency in spoken and written English, French and Dutch is required.


Additional Information



Unfortunately, we are unable to provide a work permit for this position.



Sia is an equal opportunity employer. All aspects of employment, including hiring, promotion, remuneration, or discipline, are based solely on performance, competence, conduct, or business needs.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Machine Learning Engineer




📍 Lokeren, Belgium | Full-Time | Biotech Startup




We are working with an innovative biotech startup based in Lokeren that is looking to hire a talented Machine Learning Engineer to join their growing technical team.

This is an exciting opportunity to work at the intersection of biotechnology, Machine Learning and AI, and software engineering, helping transform complex client data into scalable machine learning solutions that directly power cutting-edge software products.




You will collaborate closely with a small, highly skilled team with expertise across software development, Python engineering, and artificial intelligence.




The Role

As a Machine Learning Engineer, you will play a key role in designing and developing machine learning pipelines that convert client data into meaningful, production-ready outputs. You will be responsible for building robust data workflows, training models, and helping integrate intelligent systems into customer-facing products.




This role requires both strong technical capability and excellent communication skills, as you will work closely with both technical colleagues and external stakeholders to understand requirements and translate them into effective ML solutions.




Responsibilities

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Context

Indosuez Wealth Management is the global wealth management arm of Crédit Agricole Group, one of Europe’s largest banking groups. With over 150 years of heritage, the firm combines international reach with a human-scale approach, serving families, entrepreneurs, senior executives, and ultra-high-net-worth individuals worldwide.

It has a global presence, we are operational in 16 countries across Europe, Asia-Pacific, the Middle East, and the Americas. When joining Indosuez Wealth Management, you will inherit 4500 colleagues worldwide, which foster career opportunities within the group.

Within the IWM Center of Expertise “Data Management” based in Brussels (Degroof Petercam, the Belgian entity of the IWM group), we are currently looking for a Data Scientist.

Your position

As a Data Scientist at the CDO Office, you will support the team by delivering AI and smart automation use cases. Your main objective in this function will be to automate all sorts of data tasks within the bank. You will engage with business to look at how you can deliver them value, understand their use case, and solve it in a highly interactive process. Some cases will require advanced modelling techniques, or LLM’s; others are two regexes and an intelligent merge.

Other Tasks Include


Creating and updating of:
Documentation – Inform your stakeholders;
Dashboards – Provide Insights into your solution;
Technical Workshops - Train your users;
Process automation by scripting;
Deployment, decommissioning, and monitoring of running applications;


Your profile


You are holding a Master’s degree in computer science with an orientation with statistical knowledge
You have excellent writing skills and are fluent in English, and French or Dutch.
Your personal motto is “Show me the code”.
You’ve mastered Python
You’re interested in discovering Open Source tools (OpenWebUI), Agentic Frameworks, and Containerization.
Team spirit is in your DNA, and you like to work in a dynamic team in a challenging environment.


Our offer

For more than 150 years, Degroof Petercam has been a reference investment house. Entrepreneurship is our DNA, expertise is our fuel. You will become part of a company with highly skilled, talented and passionate experts that aim for nothing less than excellence. The impact of our work goes beyond profit by looking at business from a broader perspective, challenging those who want to intellectually. As a part of Indosuez Wealth Management and Crédit Agricole, we offer our people ongoing learning and development and mobility on an international scale.

In addition to our fascinating work environment, we also offer:


A broad offer of learning & development programs including a Degroof Petercam Academy for both professional and personal goals.
An attractive and market conform compensation package
Flexible working hours & teleworking
An Employee Social Engagement Program
Access to the same Private Banking Expertise as our clients

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
We are currently looking for a Data Scientis to strengthen the Cronos Europa team.

Location: Near-site (accessible within 2 hours from Brussels) - Remote work possible

This is NOT a full‑remote or worldwide remote position.

Remote work is strictly limited to candidates physically located within a 2‑hour distance from Brussels.

Responsabilities


Provision of expert advice and assistance, technology watch, evaluation studies, architectural considerations, applicable standards, solution patterns and best practices in following areas of expertise: Business Intelligence, Data Analytics, Artificial Intelligence.
Design, development and deployment of AI-powered applications using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and LLM-based architecture.
Development of NLP pipelines for document processing, summarization, semantic search and conversational AI
Implementation of machine learning models (supervised / unsupervised) for recommendation systems, classification and prediction
Design and optimisation of data ingestion and transformation pipelines in cloud environments (Azure, Databricks, APIs)
Data modelling, data analysis and preparation of datasets for machine learning and AI solutions
Integration of AI solutions with existing enterprise systems and data platforms
Contribution to the design of data lake architectures and ETL processes (Bronze/Silver/Gold layers)
Development of analytics and reporting solutions (e.g. Power BI dashboards)
Participation in technical working groups, progress meetings and meetings with the users.
Assistance during relevant parts of testing, analysis, technical documentation, deployment, evaluation and reporting


Technical Skills


Data Mining, data preparation for Machine Learning / Deep Learning algorithms.
Machine Learning / Deep Learning techniques in solutions development.
Natural Language Processing (NLP), including semantic search, text processing and conversational AI applications
Data science / data analytics tools and technologies such as Python, LLM frameworks like Haystack, Tensorflow, PyTorch, Jupyter Notebook, etc...
Data engineering and data processing pipelines (ETL, APIs, Azure Databricks, Spark)
Good knowledge of data modelling, data warehouse and data lake architecture
Experience with BI and reporting tools (e.g. Power BI)
Docker containerisation and software virtualisation and deployment is an asset
Knowledge of project management methodologies (Agile, Scrum) is an asset.
Good knowledge of architecture best practices and patterns.


Non-Technical Skills


Capability of integration in an international/multicultural environment, rapid self-starting capability and experience in working in team;
Ability to participate in multilingual meetings;
Ability to work in multi-cultural environment, on multiple large projects;
Excellent Team Player
Ability to understand, speak and write English (C1), French (B1) will be an advantage;
High degree of discretion and integrity is required as the applications managed and maintained in the unit could contain personal and confidential data


Spécific Expertise


at least 2 years of prompt engineering for LLMs
at least 5 years of Data / systems
at least 3 years of BI / reporting


Profile


Bachelor's or Master's degree in IT, Business Management, or a related field.
Ability to analyze complex issues and develop effective solutions.
Good communication and interpersonal skills.
Fluency in English is required, French is a strong asset.


Why Cronos Group?

We'll Propose You


An attractive salary package
A good work-life balance environment
The assurance of working in cutting-edge technologies in an entrepreneurial spirit.
The opportunity to develop your skills thanks to tailor-made training courses according to your needs
A good job in a friendly place If you wish to integrate a dynamic structure on a human scale while working with the latest technologies, don't wait anymore and join Cronos!

Title: Data Scientist
HTML:
We currently have a vacancy for a Data Scientist fluent in English, to offer his/her services as an expert will be based in Brussels, Belgium. In the context of the first assignment, the successful candidate will be integrated in the team of the company that will closely cooperate with a major client's IT team on site.

Your Tasks:

  • Collect business requirement and develop advanced data mining solutions or identify, assess and deploy relevant existing data mining, machine learning and business intelligence solution;
  • Specification and design of presentation interfaces with optimal usability/user experience;
  • Identify, collect, convert and update different data types/sets in several locations (e.g. ETL);
  • Produces data models according to specific problems statements;
  • Scripting and programming;
  • Contribute to the design and implementation of the analytics architecture and its solution stack (including performance aspects, physical design, capacity dimensions);
  • Write the different documentation associated with the tasks and liaise with other project teams as necessary to address cross-project interdependencies.



Requirements

  • University degree in IT or relevant discipline, combined with minimum 17 years of relevant working experience in IT;
  • Minimum 5 years of experience in Python/R/Bash Scripting;
  • Minimum 3 years' of specific expertise in data analysis and data visualization;
  • Expertise in the ETL processes and tools (i.e. Talend Open Studio);
  • Excellent knowledge of Perl, Matlab, R and its NLP/ML libraries (SpaCy, NLTK, scikit-learn, pandas);
  • Excellent knowledge of continuous code delivery and unit testing;
  • Good knowledge of AWS and/or Azure;
  • Good knowledge of business intelligence tools (Tableau, SAS, SAP, GoodData);
  • Good knowledge of SQL tooling (NoSQL DB, MongoDB, Hadoop, SQL);
  • Knowledge in Database Mining systems and in Big Data technologies;
  • Knowledge in one of the following areas: predictive (forecasting, recommendation), prescriptive (simulation), sentiment analysis, topic detection, social media crawling and processing, plagiarism detection, trends/anomalies detection in datasets, recommendation systems;
  • Excellent command of the English language.



Benefits

  • Cutting-Edge Tools: Work with the latest in AI, machine learning, and advanced analytics technologies.
  • Continuous Learning: Grow your expertise through challenging projects, knowledge sharing, and ongoing training.
  • Innovation Culture: Collaborate with cross-functional teams to design data-driven solutions that push boundaries.
  • Cross-Functional Influence: Collaborate with leaders across business, IT, and project teams, driving innovation and operational efficiency.

If you are seeking a career in an exciting, dynamic and multicultural international environment with exciting opportunities that will boost your career, please send us your detailed CV in English, quoting reference (18582/05/2025).

We offer a competitive remuneration (either on contract basis or remuneration with full benefits package), based on qualifications and experience. All applications will be treated as confidential.

You may also consider all our other open vacancies by visiting the career section of our web site (www.eurodyn.com) and follow us on Twitter (@EURODYN_Careers) and LinkedIn.

EUROPEAN DYNAMICS (www.eurodyn.com) is a leading European Software, Information and Communication Technologies company, operating internationally (Athens, Brussels, Luxembourg, Copenhagen, Berlin, Stockholm, London, Nicosia, Valetta, Vienna, Den Haag, Hong Kong, etc.) The company employs over 1000 engineers, IT experts and consultants (around 3% PhD, 36% MSc and 53% BSc). We design and develop software applications using integrated, state-of-the-art technology. Our current IT projects have a value exceeding 300 million EURO. EUROPEAN DYNAMICS is a renowned supplier of IT services to European Union Institutions, international organizations, European Agencies and national government Administrations in 40 countries and 4 continents.

As part of our dedication to the diversity of our workforce, we are committed to Equal Employment Opportunity without regard for race, colour, age, national origin, ethnicity, gender, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity or religion.

Title: ML Ops Engineer
Description:
Job Title: ML Ops Engineer

Location: Europe (Remote-friendly within time zone)




About Vionlabs

At Vionlabs, we’re shaping the future of content discovery through the power of AI. Our groundbreaking metadata enrichment platform helps the world’s leading streaming services, broadcasters, and media companies deliver hyper-personalized user experiences, from smarter recommendations to intuitive content search.




We combine deep tech, computer vision, and machine learning to understand content on a whole new level - analyzing emotions, pacing, and narrative structure to unlock what truly resonates with audiences.




As a fast-growing, product-driven company with global clients and a highly collaborative culture, we offer the opportunity to work at the forefront of AI innovation in media, where your ideas and engineering skills directly shape the viewing experiences of millions.




Your Role

We are looking for a proactive and experienced ML Platform Engineer to join our AI engineering team. In this role, you will take end-to-end ownership of our machine learning platform, including pipeline design, infrastructure automation, and scaling our model lifecycle. You’ll work closely with ML researchers, data engineers, and software developers to build reliable, reproducible, and scalable systems that power our core AI products.




If you enjoy building robust ML infrastructure, automating complex workflows, and enabling data science teams to move faster - this role is for you.




Your Responsibilities

Design, build, and maintain ML pipelines for training, validation, deployment and monitoring of AI models
Develop infrastructure to support reproducible and automated ML workflows (CI/CD for ML)
Collaborate with Data and ML Engineers to productize experimental models
Manage containerized environments and orchestrate workloads using tools like Kubernetes and Prefect/Kubeflow
Set up and operate scalable data processing pipelines for feature engineering and model evaluation
Implement observability for data and model quality (e.g. drift detection, performance monitoring)
Automate deployment and rollback strategies for ML models in production and at the edge
Drive best practices in ML Ops, reproducibility, and model governance
Support GPU-enabled workloads, optimizing training and inference performance
Stay up to date with trends in AI infrastructure and propose continuous improvements




Your Skills




Required Skills & Experience

3+ years of experience in ML engineering, MLOps, or platform-focused data science roles
Proven experience with ML pipeline orchestration (e.g., Kubeflow, Prefect, Airflow, or similar)
Strong understanding of containerization (Docker) and orchestration (Kubernetes)
Experience with cloud infrastructure (GCP preferred, AWS)
Proficiency in Python and ML tooling (e.g. MLflow, scikit-learn, PyTorch, TensorFlow)
Solid understanding of CI/CD concepts and infrastructure-as-code (e.g., Terraform, GitLab CI)
Familiarity with model lifecycle management, monitoring, and metadata tracking
Comfortable working in a cross-functional team with ML researchers, software engineers, and product managers
Good communication skills and an engineering mindset focused on reliability and scalability




Nice to Have

Experience with edge deployments (e.g., constrained environments, offline models)
Background in media, video processing, or computer vision
Exposure to GPU optimization, distributed training, or batch/stream data processing
Experience with data versioning tools (e.g., DVC) and experiment tracking platforms
Familiarity with model governance frameworks and responsible AI practices




Why Join Us?

Play a key role in scaling an AI-first company at the intersection of machine learning and media
Work on real-world ML infrastructure that powers personalized content experiences
A collaborative and fast-paced environment where your work has direct impact
Competitive salary and benefits
Flexible remote work model
Opportunities for professional growth in a product-focused, deep tech company

Title: Data Science and AI Expert
Description:
Company Description

Sopra Steria offers tailored, end-to-end corporate technology and software solutions to help clients make bold choices and deliver results. Successfully so! With more than 56.000 colleagues in 30 countries, we rank as Europe’s leading digital solutions provider. Some of the most successful companies in Europe rely on our technology due to our commitment to innovation, collaboration, and value in business development.

The world is how we shape it. Let’s shape it together.

Job Description

Are you passionate about using data science and AI to real-world problems? At Sopra Steria, we’re looking for an experienced and versatile professional to help lead the design and delivery of advanced analytics and AI solutions across a wide range of domainssuch as public services, utilities, healthcare, mobility, and beyond.

In this role, you will work on impactful projects from ideation to delivery. You’ll collaborate closely with both technical and business stakeholders transforming challenges into actionable insights and production-ready solutions. Whether it’s designing an optimization algorithm, building an LLM-based assistant, or delivering a forecasting pipeline, you’ll help clients unlock value from data.

We’re looking for someone who combines deep technical expertise with the maturity to take ownership, work autonomously, and guide teams and clients through the ever-evolving AI landscape.

Qualifications

We’re seeking passionate colleagues who are eager to push the boundaries of digital transformation with AI. If you recognize yourself in most of the following, we’d love to talk:


You have at least 5 years of experience in delivering real-world AI and data science solutions in production environments.
You are proficient in Python and comfortable with frameworks like scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, or similar.
You have practical experience with machine learning, and ideally exposure to domains like natural language processing, computer vision, predictive analytics, LLM’s or optimization.
You’ve worked with at least one major cloud provider (Azure, AWS, or GCP), and understand how to bring models into production securely and efficiently.
You understand how to balance model performance with business relevance, and how to translate analytical outcomes into actionable results.
You enjoy working in a team, guiding junior colleagues, and collaborating with clients to define solution architectures and technical roadmaps.
You’re comfortable interacting with stakeholders and can communicate complex concepts clearly and persuasively.
Experience in project ownership, technical leadership, or contributing to AI strategies.
You are fluent in English, and speak either Dutch or French.


What We Can Offer You


A key role in one of Europe’s leading digital transformation companies, where innovation meets real-world impact.
The opportunity to work on high-impact projects in AI and advanced analytics across industries and public institutions.
A culture of collaboration and continuous learning, supported by an expert network of consultants, architects, and engineers.
Access to our Sopra Steria Academy, with a wide range of training opportunities and certifications.
A hybrid and flexible working model, with access to offices across Belgium.
Career development opportunities both locally and internationally, within a growing Data & AI practice.


Additional Information

Our employees are known for making bold choices and delivering results. Work among high-level professionals at the forefront of corporate software solutions and innovation at Europe’s Leading Digital Service Provider.  

We offer a generous employee benefits package that includes:


A company car or mobility budget
Laptop, phone & phone subscription
A competitive salary and indefinite contract
Insurance coverage, meal vouchers, eco-cheques, and more
Continuous learning opportunities through the Sopra Steria Academy to support your career development


People are the cornerstone of our success. That’s why we aspire to be bolder together. Our goal is to build high-functioning teams and healthy team environments that inspire and help each other to deliver excellence for each of our customers.  

Excited about this job opportunity? Ready to shape the world with us? Great! We are looking forward to your application!

Some of the most successful companies in Europe rely on our technology due to our commitment to innovation, collaboration, and value in business development.

The world is how we shape it. Let’s shape it together.

Job Description

Are you passionate about using data science and AI to real-world problems? At Sopra Steria, we’re looking for an experienced and versatile professional to help lead the design and delivery of advanced analytics and AI solutions across a wide range of domainssuch as public services, utilities, healthcare, mobility, and beyond.

In this role, you will work on impactful projects from ideation to delivery. You’ll collaborate closely with both technical and business stakeholders transforming challenges into actionable insights and production-ready solutions. Whether it’s designing an optimization algorithm, building an LLM-based assistant, or delivering a forecasting pipeline, you’ll help clients unlock value from data.

We’re looking for someone who combines deep technical expertise with the maturity to take ownership, work autonomously, and guide teams and clients through the ever-evolving AI landscape.

Qualifications

We’re seeking passionate colleagues who are eager to push the boundaries of digital transformation with AI. If you recognize yourself in most of the following, we’d love to talk:

  • You have at least 5 years of experience in delivering real-world AI and data science solutions in production environments.
  • You are proficient in Python and comfortable with frameworks like scikit-learn, TensorFlow, PyTorch, or similar.
  • You have practical experience with machine learning, and ideally exposure to domains like natural language processing, computer vision, predictive analytics, LLM’s or optimization.
  • You’ve worked with at least one major cloud provider (Azure, AWS, or GCP), and understand how to bring models into production securely and efficiently.
  • You understand how to balance model performance with business relevance, and how to translate analytical outcomes into actionable results.
  • You enjoy working in a team, guiding junior colleagues, and collaborating with clients to define solution architectures and technical roadmaps.
  • You’re comfortable interacting with stakeholders and can communicate complex concepts clearly and persuasively.
  • Experience in project ownership, technical leadership, or contributing to AI strategies.
  • You are fluent in English, and speak either Dutch or French.

What We Can Offer You

  • A key role in one of Europe’s leading digital transformation companies, where innovation meets real-world impact.
  • The opportunity to work on high-impact projects in AI and advanced analytics across industries and public institutions.
  • A culture of collaboration and continuous learning, supported by an expert network of consultants, architects, and engineers.
  • Access to our Sopra Steria Academy, with a wide range of training opportunities and certifications.
  • A hybrid and flexible working model, with access to offices across Belgium.
  • Career development opportunities both locally and internationally, within a growing Data & AI practice.

Additional Information

Our employees are known for making bold choices and delivering results. Work among high-level professionals at the forefront of corporate software solutions and innovation at Europe’s Leading Digital Service Provider.  

We offer a generous employee benefits package that includes:

  • A company car or mobility budget
  • Laptop, phone & phone subscription
  • A competitive salary and indefinite contract
  • Insurance coverage, meal vouchers, eco-cheques, and more
  • Continuous learning opportunities through the Sopra Steria Academy to support your career development

People are the cornerstone of our success. That’s why we aspire to be bolder together. Our goal is to build high-functioning teams and healthy team environments that inspire and help each other to deliver excellence for each of our customers.  

Excited about this job opportunity? Ready to shape the world with us? Great! We are looking forward to your application! 

______

Sopra Steria is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to age, ancestry, nationality, color, family or medical leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, gender (including pregnancy), sexual orientation or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. We foster a work environment that is inclusive and respectful of all differences.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
🚨 Machine Learning Engineer (Implementation)

📍 Remote / London HQ




I’m working with a fast-growing AI startup building AI-powered Virtual Agents that automate complex operational workflows directly inside customer systems. Their platform is helping businesses deploy practical AI quickly - without heavy integrations, long onboarding cycles, or major process changes.




Backed by leading AI investors and already working with major customers across insurance, healthcare, and marketplaces, they’re now hiring ML Implementation Engineers to help scale deployments and customer impact.




💡 Why this role stands out:

• Blend of software engineering, ML systems, and customer-facing work

• High ownership with direct real-world impact

• Build and deploy practical AI products, not just prototypes

• Work across infrastructure, deployment, MLOps, and AI services

• Fast-moving environment with strong product influence




🧠 You’ll:

• Work directly with customers to automate workflows using AI agents

• Deploy AI solutions into real operational environments

• Improve reliability, observability, and deployment infrastructure

• Contribute to the core AI agent platform and tooling

• Help shape how ML and computer vision systems are implemented at scale




🔧 Looking for:

• Strong software engineering fundamentals

• Exposure to ML systems, infrastructure, deployment, or MLOps

• Comfortable solving ambiguous problems with customers

• Engineers who enjoy ownership, fast iteration, and practical AI deployment




You don’t need experience across every area to be a fit. The team is open to engineers from a range of backgrounds who enjoy building useful AI systems and working closely with customers.

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Description:
Voor het Agentschap Vlaamse Dienst voor Arbeidsbemiddeling en Beroepsopleiding (VDAB), is Vlaanderen connect op zoek naar een:

Senior Data Scientist

Contract onbepaalde duur

Brussel

///////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////////

Bij VDAB geloven we dat data en artificiële intelligentie de motor zijn van een moderne arbeidsmarkt. VDAB heeft daarom een matuur AI Center of Excellence (“AI CoE”) uitgebouwd, dat een centrale rol speelt in het vormgeven en begeleiden van deze digitale evolutie.

Voor dit AI CoE zoekt VDAB een ervaren Data Scientist, die leiding en coaching geeft aan het team van Data Scientists, en die de fundamenten van het AI Center of Excellence mee bewaakt en uitbouwt.










Waarom voor deze functie kiezen?

VDAB is een voortrekker in het toepassen van artificiële intelligentie binnen de publieke sector en wil deze positie in de komende jaren verder versterken.

Je krijgt de kans om te werken in een vooruitstrevende en innovatieve omgeving die niet alleen technisch uitdagend is, maar je kan ook een directe maatschappelijke meerwaarde creëren. De oplossingen die we samen realiseren, ondersteunen burgers in elke fase van hun loopbaan en dragen tastbaar bij aan een inclusieve, efficiënte en toekomstgerichte arbeidsmarkt.

Kortom, deze rol biedt een unieke combinatie van technologische innovatie, strategische impact en maatschappelijke relevantie.







Jouw verantwoordelijkheden

Je bent succesvol in deze rol wanneer je:

We verwachten van de kandidaat dat hij/zij zelfstandig van begin tot einde een AI model kan opzetten. Dit dus gaande van het samenstellen van de data, data analyse, kiezen van het correcte model, evaluatie van de output en ondersteuning bij het in productie zetten van dit model. Daarnaast verwachten we dat de kandidaat ook in staat is om zijn/haar keuzes uit te leggen aan een publiek met niet-technische achtergrond. Dit zowel op basis van een presentatie als op basis van een rapport.

Daarnaast verwachten we dat je leiding en coaching geeft aan het team van Data Scientists waarbij je :

* een sterke vertrouwensband opbouwt met de AI Data Scientists

* hen ondersteunt als coach maar ook uitdaagt om het beste uit zichzelf te halen in het belang van de organisatiedoelen

* een volledig overzicht hebt van de huidige AI portfolio en de lopende AI projecten, en de kritieke pijnpunten op vlak van stabiliteit en onderhoudbaarheid kent

* duidelijke afspraken maakt over o.a. code reviews, testing (unit/integratie) en documentatievereisten voor alle nieuwe AI-projecten, in het kader van de AI Governance van VDAB

* bijdraagt aan een geïntegreerde en performante samenwerking van Projectleiders, Data Scientists en Data Engineers

* bijdraagt aan de strategie en visie van het AI Center of Excellence

* bijdraagt aan een goede werking en goede relaties met andere teams zowel binnen als buiten I&T







De 3 belangrijkste verantwoordelijkheden :

Je stuurt en coacht een performant team van Data Scientists. Je zorgt daarbij voor een cultuur van continue verbetering, kennisdeling en technologische innovatie.
Je vertaalt business problemen naar ML/AI oplossingen en je ontwikkelt mee AI/ML modellen van experiment tot productie.
Je bewaakt mee de roadmap en de stabiliteit van de AI-applicaties







Wat zijn de belangrijkste taken?

Ontwikkelen van AI/ML/LLM modellen
Coachen en challengen van de meest geschikte oplossingen (bv ML/ LLM) gegeven de uitdaging
Delen van kennis en inzichten van ontwikkelde modellen







Wat zijn je technische vaardigheden ?







Master of gelijkwaardig door ervaring
Diepgaande kennis van statistiek/probabiliteitstheorie
Diepgaande kennis van Machine Learning Algoritmes.
Uitgebreide ervaring met Python en (Py)Spark
Uitgebreide ervaring met GIT of andere code repositories
Uitgebreide ervaring met generatieve AI toepassingen en modellen
Ervaring met NLP is een pluspunt
Ervaring met Deep Learning is een pluspunt
Ervaring met het in productie zetten van ML oplossingen is een pluspunt
Op de hoogte zijn van de laatste evoluties op het vlak van AI




Welke zijn je persoonlijkheid vaardigheden ?

* Teamplayer - gericht op samenwerking zowel met business teams als technische teams

* People management en ervaring met coachend leiderschap

* Resultaatgericht

* Kritische en oplossingsgerichte denker

* Bewezen ervaring met stakeholder management

* Bewezen ervaring in constructief omgaan met conflicterende visies

* Ervaring met innovatie




Waar ga je werken ?

Je werkt 2 dagen in Brussel, op wandelafstand van het station Brussel Noord.




Jouw toekomstig team

Het team waarvoor je verantwoordelijk bent, bestaat momenteel uit 10 teamleden, waar we de rollen van junior, medior en senior Data Scientist terugvinden. Je werkt nauw samen met een Manager AI Engineering en de Portfolio Manager die het AI Portfolio beheert en de Project Managers aanstuurt. En uiteraard met de Manager van het AI Center of Excellence, aan wie je rapporteert.




Wat zijn typische projecten van deze functie/afdeling?




Typische projecten ontstaan vanuit een idee (ter ondersteuning van burger, werkgever of medewerkers van VDAB) afkomstig van binnen in het team of van business. Hierbij ligt de focus altijd op kwaliteits- en/of efficiëntieverhoging. We starten hier rond een experiment om om te kijken welke meerwaarde we kunnen bieden met behulp van data en AI. Indien dit positief verloopt, wordt het model in meer detail uitgewerkt en wordt er een testbaar prototype ontwikkeld. De volgende stap is dan het in productie plaatsen van de applicatie.







Wat jij van ons mag verwachten




Vlaanderen connect biedt een contract van 38 uur voor onbepaalde duur en een competitief salarispakket met:

Een aantrekkelijke bruto verloning
Een bonussysteem op basis van vast te leggen doelstellingen
28 dagen verlof en extra wettelijke feestdagen (11/07, 02/11 en 15/11).
Een mobiliteitsbudget
Maaltijdcheques van 8 euro/dag (eigen bijdrage 1,09 euro/dag)
Een forfaitaire onkostenvergoeding
Een hospitalisatieverzekering (je kan gezinsleden aansluiten)
Een groepsverzekering
Een arbeidsongevallenverzekering
Een opleidingsbudget
Een laptop
Een smartphone met abonnementsformule
Flexibele werkuren
Thuiswerk Middelen en mogelijkheden tot thuiswerk
Tegemoetkoming in de kosten van school vakantieopvang
Show more

Title: AI Engineer
Description:
Company Description

Sopra Steria offers tailored, end-to-end corporate technology and software solutions to help clients make bold choices and deliver results. Successfully so! With more than 51.000 colleagues in nearly 30 countries, we rank as Europe’s leading digital solutions provider. Some of the most successful companies in Europe rely on our technology due to our commitment to innovation, collaboration, and value in business development.

The world is how we shape it. Let’s shape it together.

Job Description

As an AI Engineer, you bring traditional and Generative AI into real world use cases by helping our clients design, build, and serve Generative AI solutions. You design and implement solutions that require from traditional AI to LLMs, from semantic search to conversational AI. You train, fine-tune, improve, and deploy ML models. Furthermore, you write production ready code to serve online, batch, and real time models. You build applications and software to serve AI driven use cases.

You work in close collaboration with Data Scientists, MLOps Engineers and Data Engineers, to integrate all parts of the solution. You help build solutions and/or operate with our clients on a medium to long-term basis.

Qualifications

We’re seeking passionate colleagues who are eager to push the boundaries in AI consulting. At Sopra Steria, you’ll have the opportunity to grow your skills in a constructive, collaborative team environment, working on impactful projects that drive change for our clients. You will be part of the Data & AI team, where we tackle AI challenges end to end, from strategy, governance, to science and engineering. If you thrive on challenge and meet (most of) the qualifications below, we look forward to your application!

You’ll have knowledge and experience of the following:


At least 3 years of experience as a Machine Learning Engineer, Data Scientist, MLOps Engineer, or in a similar position
Experience with traditional NLP, LLMs, and conversational AI, both in experimentation phase and in production
Experience with vector databases for semantic search and RAG solutions in a production environment.
Strong software engineering background and ability to write production ready code in Python
Experience with experiment tracking, models monitoring, LLM and NLP evaluation techniques, and deployment strategies
Comfortable with the machine learning lifecycle and MLOps and DevOps principles
Experience with at least one cloud provider and good knowledge of the data ecosystem
Able to coach others, give technical advice and direction, and work independently
Master or PhD in Machine Learning, Artificial Intelligence, Computer Engineering, or related field
Proficient in English and you're also fluent in Dutch and/or French


What We Can Offer You

Become part of a major Tech player in Europe that is recognised for consulting, digital services and software development. Work among high-level professionals who dare to make bold choices to deliver results.

Additionally, you’ll join our Data & AI team, where we support enterprises and institutions to become more data driven, and assist our clients to increase business efficiency and create impact with data. We offer a complete portfolio of Services and Solutions to unburden organisations in every step of their transition: from defining and executing the data strategy and governance, over setting up data platforms, to visualizing dashboards and applying the latest data science and artificial intelligence methodologies.  

Additional Information

Are you ready to further develop your career? Then we’d love to point you in the right direction. With offices in more than 30 countries, that direction might lead you to places you’ve never been to before. Would you prefer to grow internally? That’s possible, too. We challenge you to keep pushing yourself to become the best you can be.

We offer a generous employee benefits package that includes:


A variety of perks, such as mobility options (including a company car), insurance coverage, meal vouchers, eco-cheques, and more.
Continuous learning opportunities through the Sopra Steria Academy to support your career development.
The opportunity to connect with fellow Sopra Steria colleagues at various team events.


Ready to take the next step?

We’re here to support you all the way and we are looking forward to your application!

______

Sopra Steria is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will be considered for employment without regard to age, ancestry, nationality, color, family or medical leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, gender (including pregnancy), sexual orientation or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. We foster a work environment that is inclusive and respectful of all differences.
