Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Who We Are

We are a leader in fraud prevention and AML compliance. Our platform uses device intelligence, behavior biometrics, machine learning, and AI to stop fraud before it happens. Today, over 300 banks, retailers, and fintechs worldwide use Sardine to stop identity fraud, payment fraud, account takeovers, and social engineering scams. We have raised $145M from world-class investors, including Andreessen Horowitz, Activant, Visa, Experian, FIS, and Google Ventures.

Our Culture


We have hubs in the Bay Area, NYC, Austin, Toronto, and São Paulo. However, we maintain a remote-first work culture. #WorkFromAnywhere
We hire talented, self-motivated individuals with extreme ownership and high growth orientation.
We value performance and not hours worked. We believe you shouldn't have to miss your family dinner, your kid's school play, friends get-together, or doctor's appointments for the sake of adhering to an arbitrary work schedule.


Location


UK, Germany, Ireland, Spain, Poland, Bulgaria and Lithuania - Remote
From Home / Beach / Mountain / Cafe / Anywhere!
We are a remote-first company with a globally distributed team. So you can find your productive zone and work from there.


About The Role

We're looking for a data-driven professional to help us measure, understand, and improve the performance of our risk strategies — and to stay ahead of evolving fraud threats by designing and deploying data-driven solutions with real-world impact. You'll work directly with clients to understand their unique fraud challenges, rapidly prototype proof-of-concept models, and build scalable, production-ready solutions using machine learning and graph analytics.

You'll also analyze complex datasets, design metrics, build dashboards, and collaborate closely with stakeholders across the business to drive decision-making and optimize outcomes.

This is a hands-on, high-impact role ideal for someone who thrives at the intersection of data science, client-facing problem solving, and real-time risk.

What You'll Be Doing


Champion a data-first approach across internal teams and client engagements, promoting clarity and impact
Build and deploy machine learning models to prevent fraud across diverse fintech use cases, from proof-of-concept through to production
Develop and track metrics to measure and monitor the performance of our risk products and the effectiveness of risk management strategies
Conduct in-depth analyses to uncover insights contributing to fraud reduction and higher approval rates for our clients
Work directly with clients to understand their fraud challenges and translate complex data insights into clear, actionable recommendations
Use data and models to support the development of risk mitigation strategies and interventions while preserving and improving the user experience
Create and automate self-serve dashboards leveraging BI tools
Collaborate with engineering to scale models into production, optimize performance, and support data instrumentation
Partner with cross-functional teams (Business, Product, and Engineering) to translate business requirements into data-driven solutions


What You'll Need


7+ years of experience in data science, quantitative modeling, or a data-focused role (product analytics, business analytics) with demonstrated high impact in fraud or risk contexts
Strong hands-on experience with Python/R and SQL is essential, with Spark being a nice to have
Expertise in BI tools such as Tableau, Sigma, or Metabase
Proven ability to structure and analyze complex data using techniques like EDA and cohort analysis, and communicate findings effectively to both technical and non-technical audiences, including clients
Sharp critical thinking and creative problem-solving skills with a bias toward action
Proficiency in defining, tracking, and communicating performance metrics


Benefits We Offer


Generous compensation in cash and equity
Early exercise for all options, including pre-vested
Work from anywhere: Remote-first Culture
Flexible paid time off and Year-end break
Health insurance, dental, and vision coverage for employees and dependents - US and Canada specific
4% matching in 401k / RRSP - US and Canada specific
MacBook Pro delivered to your door
One-time stipend to set up a home office — desk, chair, screen, etc.
Monthly meal stipend
Monthly social meet-up stipend
Annual health and wellness stipend
Annual Learning stipend


Join a fast-growing company with world-class professionals from around the world. If you are seeking a meaningful career, you found the right place, and we would love to hear from you.

To learn more about how we process your personal information and your rights in regards to your personal information as an applicant and Sardine employee, please visit our Applicant and Worker Privacy Notice.

Title: Data Scientist - Waterloo, Belgium (F/M)
Description:
What we will accomplish together:

Alsinova is looking on a regular basis for Data Scientist Consultants to join our consulting team for clients' projects (from big pharma to small biotech) based in Belgium.

Your mission (should you decide to accept it):

Your potential challenges will be various, depending on the clients' priorities. The list below gives you examples of our classic activities.

Your daily activities will be:


Use of advanced analytics technologies, including machine learning and predictive modeling.
Gathering and analyzing data, using various types of analytics and reporting tools to detect patterns, trends and relationships in data sets.
Use statistical approaches to solve analytical problems.
Develop custom systems (hardware and software) internally and with external partners.
Work in various projects/therapeutic areas: neurosciences, oncology, in vivo and in vitro research activities, genomics, patient follow-ups, safety and risk management, etc.
Work in close interaction with stakeholders to install and implement specific equipment, tools and systems to support.
Provide documentation and procedures to other stakeholders.
Provide activities planning.
Project management and cost reporting.


You:


Engineering or biologist degree, data scientist.
At least 5 years of experience as a data scientist, or computer scientist, or database and software programmer. Knowledge of :
Microsoft office.
Programming languages (C, MATLAB, R, Python) and visualization tools (PowerBI, Tableau)
Signal processing.
Statistics and algorithms.
AI, machine learning, deep learning knowledge, big data.
Database knowledge (SQL, Oracle).
English and French fluent.


Our Benefits

Joining Astek means choosing:


an experience accelerator, where every assignment is an opportunity to progress
access to a multitude of technically exciting projects for our clients as well as internally
individualized, local support for a tailored career path
continuous learning, thanks to our internal training academy
a friendly and inclusive work environment, supported by #TopEmployer2026 certified HR practices Let's move forward.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
ML OPS Engineer / AI Engineer

About NORRIQ

NORRIQ is an international group based in Denmark and Belgium, comprising over 350 consultants dedicated to unlocking the full business potential of companies through technology and digital transformation.

We have a specialized Financial Services practice that is experiencing rapid growth. Over 100 consultants focus exclusively on the financial services sector, assisting banks and insurances in redesigning their operations to meet changing client expectations, adopt new technologies, and adhere to evolving regulations (DORA, RIS, AI Act, AML, KYC, etc).

At NORRIQ, our employees are at the heart of our organization and our success.. We hire and nurture passionate individuals who strive for excellence for both our clients and colleagues. Our culture supports personal and professional growth with training, opportunities, and creative freedom. Regardless of when you join or how long you stay, you'll experience an unforgettable NORRIQ journey and become a part of our extended family. Connected forever!

Role Overview

As an MLOps Engineer / AI Engineer, you will play a central role in bridging Data Science and IT Operations. You will design, deploy, and scale production-grade machine learning and AI solutions for our financial services clients. Your mission is to ensure robust, secure, and efficient AI operations—covering deployment, observability, lifecycle management, and continuous improvement.

This role is ideal for someone who loves building AI infrastructure, optimizing performance, and enabling data scientists to move faster and safer.

Key Responsibilities


Deploy ML/AI Models: Work with Data Scientists to deploy secure, scalable, and reliable models into production.
Build CI/CD Pipelines: Automate testing, packaging, and deployment for data and model workflows.
Manage Data Pipelines: Design and maintain efficient data flows across storage systems, databases, and platforms.
Set Up Environments: Manage dependencies, development environments, and tooling for smooth project onboarding.
Operate Kubernetes: Deploy and maintain containerized AI components using Kubernetes.
Monitor & Troubleshoot: Oversee model/application performance, resolve incidents, and conduct root-cause analyses.
Optimize & Innovate: Identify improvements, adopt new technologies, and enhance AI engineering practices.


Requirements

Education


Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related field.
Strong background in programming, system engineering, or DevOps is highly valued.


Technical Skills


Strong proficiency in Python.
Experience with REST APIs and Git.
Hands-on expertise in CI/CD and MLOps/DevOps practices.
Practical experience with Docker and Kubernetes.
Familiarity with Linux, software testing, and modern software engineering workflows.
Nice to have: SQL/PostgreSQL, MLflow, Helm, or related tooling.


Soft Skills


Collaborative and team-oriented mindset.
Proactive, solution-driven, and comfortable working in dynamic environments.
Strong communication, analytical, and problem-solving abilities.


Languages


Fluent in English (spoken and written).
Knowledge of additional languages (Dutch or French) is a plus.


Why Joining Us?


You will be part of a fast growing international company with a unique people culture
A very competitive salary package including a company car, iPhone and different allowances
You will get extensive trainings in Belgium and Denmark on technical matters, as well as soft skills and project management, and you will have access to new technologies and innovative equipment.
You will join a dynamic and growing team with a great mix of young and experienced professionals focusing on financial services.

Title: Data Engineer · Belgique · CDI ou Freelance
Description:
MindScout est un cabinet de recrutement et conseil Data en Belgique.




Le recrutement ne suit pas le rythme des évolutions du monde du travail. Les CVs se ressemblent tous, optimisés par IA, mêmes buzzwords, mêmes projets. Les entretiens récompensent plus les bons pitchers que ceux qui délivrent. Et personne ne mesure ce qui compte vraiment : le jugement, les arbitrages, la capacité à orchestrer les bons outils au bon moment.

On a construit MindScout pour changer ça. On ne vend pas de CVs. On évalue comment les gens travaillent, et on les met en relation avec les bonnes missions.

On construit notre réseau de Data Engineers évalués. Si tu travailles en data en Belgique (Bruxelles, Wallonie, ou remote pour un client belge), cette annonce est pour toi.




Ce n'est pas une offre pour un poste unique. C'est un accès à un réseau de missions Data (CDI et freelance) auprès d'entreprises qui recrutent autrement.




Comment ça marche :

Tu passes un parcours structuré de moins d'une heure, en ligne, quand tu veux. Pas un test technique mais plutôt une mise en situation réelle : un brief data, des contraintes qui bougent, des décisions à prendre. On observe comment tu structures, priorises et réagis. Ensuite, un call avec ton recruteur MindScout pour discuter de ce que tu cherches et de ce qui t'anime.




Ce que tu obtiens :

→ Un Identity Profile : un bilan détaillé de ta façon de travailler, généré automatiquement. Tes forces, tes axes de progression, ton rapport à l'IA. Un document que tu gardes et que tu peux partager sur LinkedIn.

→ Un accès aux missions Data qu'on gère en Belgique. Quand ton profil matche un besoin client, tu es contacté en priorité.

→ Aucune préparation requise. Aucun frais. Le parcours est gratuit et confidentiel.




Profil recherché :

On ne recrute pas des stacks, on recrute des builders. Si tu construis de la data au quotidien (pipelines, transformations, orchestration, qualité) et que tu es en Belgique ou disponible pour des missions belges, on veut voir comment tu travailles.




Pour démarrer : postule ici ou rends toi sur app.mindscout.io/apply

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
🚀 Data Scientist (Computer Vision & NLP) | Relocate to Porto, Portugal | 1–4 Years Experience




Hey future innovator 👋

We’re on the hunt for a Data Scientist who’s obsessed with solving real-world problems using Computer Vision, NLP, and Deep Learning. If you’ve got 1–4 years of experience, right to work in EU, and a passion for building cool stuff with Python, we want to hear from you.




**You must be willing to relocate to Porto, Portugal - full relocation support**




Why work in iGaming with US?

Because you'll be building cutting-edge tech that powers entertainment for millions—where innovation meets impact, and your work directly shapes the future of digital gaming experiences.




What you’ll be doing:

Building smart ML models using image, text, audio & tabular data
Training pipelines in Azure and deploying end-to-end apps in Python
Creating tools for model inference, logging, and performance tracking
Collaborating with data and engineering teams to solve complex challenges
Staying ahead of the curve with the latest in LLMs, RAG, and CV/NLP trends




What you bring:

Solid Python skills + experience with PyTorch or TensorFlow
Hands-on with Docker, CI/CD, and testing frameworks like Pytest
Experience with model tracking, versioning, and performance metrics
Bonus points if you’ve worked with Azure ML, Huggingface, or LangChain




What’s in it for you:

💸 Competitive salary
📚 Training on industry best practices
🌍 Remote-first culture with a global team
🚀 Growth opportunities in a fast-moving tech company




If you’re ready to level up your career and build cutting-edge solutions with a team that values innovation, apply now! 🌟




Remote (EU only) | Must have right to work

Title: Data Scientist H/F
Description:
MCA BELGIUM, filiale du groupe français MCA, est une société de conseil en ingénierie et hautes technologies. Avec plus de 15 ans d’expérience en Belgique, nous accompagnons nos clients dans la réalisation de projets industriels complexes dans divers secteurs d’activité (énergie, nucléaire, pharmaceutique, aéronautique, ferroviaire).

Nous rejoindre, c’est intégrer une entreprise dynamique qui place l’accompagnement de ses collaborateurs au cœur de ses priorités. Vous bénéficierez d’un suivi personnalisé et de nombreuses formations pour développer vos compétences.

Votre Rôle

Rattaché(e) au pôle Data & Engineering, vous intervenez sur l’exploitation avancée de données industrielles issues d’équipements d’énergies renouvelables (solaire & éolien), avec pour objectif d’optimiser la performance des installations et d’anticiper les défaillances :


Analyse de données provenant de machines industrielles (production, capteurs, historiques de fonctionnement)
Conception et développement de modèles statistiques et algorithmiques (machine learning, détection d’anomalies)
Identification des pertes de performance, anomalies et risques de panne
Participation à la mise en place de solutions de maintenance prédictive
Contribution à l’optimisation du potentiel des équipements
Restitution des résultats via rapports techniques et documentation associée
Collaboration avec les équipes multidisciplinaires (ingénierie, experts terrain, métiers)
Veille sur les évolutions technologiques et méthodologiques en data science appliquée à l’industrie


Votre Profil


Diplôme d’ingénieur ou formation équivalente, idéalement avec une spécialisation en data science, mathématiques appliquées ou domaine proche
Première expérience professionnelle en data (stage long, VIE ou premier poste accepté), avec exposition concrète à des projets data
Maîtrise de Python
Bonne compréhension des algorithmes, statistiques et approches machine learning
Esprit analytique, rigueur et sens du travail en équipe
Maîtrise du français & anglais professionnel


Vos Perspectives D’évolution

Nous souhaitons vous accompagner dans votre montée en compétences. Notre objectif est de vous donner toutes les clés pour réussir et accéder aux différentes opportunités d’évolution. Vous pourrez évoluer vers des fonctions techniques, de gestion de projet ou de management. Ce poste est ouvert aux personnes en situation de handicap.

  • Collaboration avec les équipes multidisciplinaires (ingénierie, experts terrain, métiers)
  • Veille sur les évolutions technologiques et méthodologiques en data science appliquée à l’industrie

Votre Profil

  • Diplôme d’ingénieur ou formation équivalente, idéalement avec une spécialisation en data science, mathématiques appliquées ou domaine proche
  • Première expérience professionnelle en data (stage long, VIE ou premier poste accepté), avec exposition concrète à des projets data
  • Maîtrise de Python
  • Bonne compréhension des algorithmes, statistiques et approches machine learning
  • Esprit analytique, rigueur et sens du travail en équipe
  • Maîtrise du français & anglais professionnel

Vos Perspectives D’évolution

Nous souhaitons vous accompagner dans votre montée en compétences. Notre objectif est de vous donner toutes les clés pour réussir et accéder aux différentes opportunités d’évolution. Vous pourrez évoluer vers des fonctions techniques, de gestion de projet ou de management. Ce poste est ouvert aux personnes en situation de handicap.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer (BE)
Description:
Job Description

At CluePoints, we’re redefining how clinical trials are run. As the premier provider of Risk-Based Quality Management (RBQM) and Data Quality Oversight software, we harness advanced statistics, artificial intelligence, and machine learning to ensure the quality, accuracy, and integrity of clinical trial data, helping life sciences organizations bring safer, more effective treatments to patients faster.

We’re proud to be an ambitious, fast-growing technology scale-up with a dynamic and diverse international team representing more than 40 nationalities. Collaboration, flexibility, and continuous learning are part of our DNA.

At CluePoints, you’ll find a culture where you can grow, make an impact, and have fun along the way. Guided by our values of Care, Passion, and Smart Disruption, we’re united by a shared mission: to create smarter ways to run efficient clinical trials and deliver AI-powered insights that improve human outcomes worldwide.

We are looking for an AI Engineer to join our AI Innovation team, focused on exploring, prototyping, and operationalizing cutting-edge AI solutions for clinical data review. This role sits at the intersection of research and engineering: you will investigate emerging AI capabilities—particularly LLMs and agentic AI systems—and translate them into impactful use cases within clinical trials. While the primary focus is on innovation and experimentation, you are expected to drive selected prototypes through to robust, production-ready solutions.

Job Requirements

Main Qualifications


Master’s or PhD in Computer Science, AI, Machine Learning, or a related quantitative field
Strong programming skills in Python
Proven experience working with LLMs and modern AI frameworks (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic, Mistral, Meta)
Solid understanding of agentic AI concepts and orchestration frameworks (e.g.,
LangGraph, AutoGen, CrewAI, function calling)
Experience with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) and/or hybrid AI systems
Strong software engineering fundamentals (modular design, APIs, version control with Git)


Preferred Qualifications (Nice to have)


Experience with clinical trial data (protocols, EDC, CTMS, AE/SAE reporting)
Familiarity with regulated environments (e.g., life sciences, healthcare)
Experience deploying AI/ML systems into production environments
Knowledge of vector databases, embeddings, and information retrieval systems
Background in NLP, deep learning, or applied AI research
Experience with data quality, statistical methods, or risk-based monitoring
Familiarity with GPU computing and performance optimization


Job Responsibilities

AI Innovation & Exploration


Explore and evaluate emerging AI techniques, with a focus on LLMs and agentic AI systems
Design and prototype novel AI-driven approaches for clinical data review (e.g., protocol interpretation, query generation, deviation detection, medical coding)
Translate scientific literature and industry trends into practical experimentation and prototypes
Identify new opportunities where AI can bring value within clinical research workflows


Agent & System Development


Architect and build intelligent agent systems leveraging LLMs, RAG, and tool augmentation
Integrate diverse data sources (e.g., protocols, clinical datasets, operational metadata) into AI workflows
Develop modular services, APIs, and reusable components to support AI applications
Prototype interactive interfaces (e.g., chat-based agents, copilots, APIs)


From Prototype to Production


Contribute to the industrialization of selected AI solutions, ensuring scalability and robustness
Apply best practices for evaluation, monitoring, and reproducibility of AI systems
Ensure outputs are traceable, auditable, and aligned with regulatory expectations
Collaborate with engineering teams to transition prototypes into production-ready systems


Collaboration & Thought Leadership


Work closely with clinical experts, product managers, and R&D teams to refine use cases
Communicate findings, trade-offs, and recommendations clearly to stakeholders
Stay at the forefront of AI advancements and contribute to CluePoints’ innovation strategy
Optionally contribute to publications, conferences, or internal knowledge sharing


Job Benefits

🇧🇪 What We Offer – Belgium


Health Insurance through Alan (100% hospitalisation cover, 80% ambulatory and dental)
Mobility Budget for eco-transport, housing, or car allowance (flexible 3-pillar system)
Group Insurance Plan with 6–12% employer pension contribution based on seniority
Meal Vouchers (€8/day) and Eco Vouchers for sustainable purchases
A hub-based hybrid model that blends flexibility with purpose — connecting teams through collaboration, learning, and a vibrant social culture.


Equal Opportunities & GDPR Notice

CluePoints is an equal opportunities employer. We value and respect diversity in our workforce and do not tolerate discrimination based on gender, age, disability, ethnic origin, religion, sexual orientation, or any other protected ground under Belgian law.

Personal data collected as part of your application will be processed in compliance with the EU GDPR and Belgian data protection legislation.

You have the right to access, correct, or delete your personal data at any time by contacting privacy@cluepoints.com.

Title: Lead Scientist
Description:
Ready to architect the future of human-computer voice interaction?




Join an established conversational AI company as they transition from traditional cascaded speech systems to cutting-edge E2E speech-to-speech technology. You'll lead this transformation, building multimodal systems that will redefine how millions interact with AI.




The opportunity

You'll be leading the development of speech technology that directly impacts real users at massive scale. The company processes millions of daily interactions across major enterprise clients, meaning your research will shape real-world conversational experiences.




You'll spearhead the development of full-duplex speech systems, creating truly natural AI conversations that go far beyond current capabilities.




Your impact

Design and build next-generation multimodal speech LLM architecture from the ground up
Drive breakthroughs in speech-to-speech modeling and full-duplex conversation systems
Tackle turn-taking, interruption handling, and simultaneous speech processing
Bridge cutting-edge research with enterprise-grade production systems
Lead a growing team focused on SOTA speech-to-speech breakthroughs and own the development end-to-end




What you'll bring

Deep understanding of SOTA speech models and neural audio processing
Experience building speech language models/multimodal systems
Strong background in speech AI research and modern speech architectures




This is all underpinned by access to a large corpus of real enterprise conversational data and serious GPU infrastructure.




The company has built everything in-house, giving you complete technical control and the freedom to explore any approach that delivers value.




With their established market position and proven track record, you'll have the resources and real-world testing ground to make transformative impact with your research.




Location

Remote (Must be within EU timezone).

Title: AI Tester
Description:
Nous recherchons actuellement un(e) Software Quality Assurance Tester pour intervenir sur une plateforme technologique innovante, développée dans un environnement agile, orienté rapidité d'exécution, qualité et amélioration continue, avec l'AI comme véritable alliée du quotidien.

🎯 Le contexte

Environnement dynamique et pragmatique
Forte orientation delivery et mise en production rapide
Culture d'expérimentation : tester, apprendre, ajuster
Utilisation d'agents AI pour accélérer et optimiser le travail
Collaboration étroite entre QA, développeurs et produit
Approche ouverte : capacité à remettre en question ou abandonner une solution si nécessaire

🧪 Votre rôle En tant que QA Tester, vous serez un acteur clé de la qualité :

Création, maintenance et exécution de tests fonctionnels et de régression
Tests d'APIs (Postman ou équivalent), définition des payloads et analyse des réponses JSON
Tests de performance et de charge (API & front-end)
Tests d'interfaces web et applications fat client
Contribution aux analyses fonctionnelles, grooming, rétrospectives et démos
Travail avec des dashboards (Kibana, Grafana) et mise en place d'alerting
Revue des alertes, warnings et messages d'erreur en lien avec le service delivery

👤 Profil recherché

Expérience en test manuel et automatisé
Bonne connaissance de Jira, Selenium, LoadRunner, Postman, SoapUI, GitLab CI/CD
À l'aise avec les bases de données (Mongo, DB2, Oracle)
Esprit analytique, logique et critique
Curiosité, sens du service et bon esprit d'équipe
Français obligatoire + anglais technique au quotidien

📍 Infos pratiques

Localisation : Bruxelles et/ou Fleurus
Démarrage : 16 juin
Statut : permanent

👉 Si ce rôle vous parle, n'hésitez pas à envoyer votre cv actualisé.
Vous pouvez également partager cette opportunité avec votre network.




To find out more about Huxley please visit www.huxley.com

Title: Medior Data Scientist
Description:
Are you passionate about #AI or #cybersecurity? You love working with high profile teams with a sure taste for challenge and variety? You dream to join a fast-growing company with start-up mentality? Eager to learn continuously? Join Proximus Ada! Within Proximus’ centre of excellence for AI and Cybersecurity, a team of data scientists, data engineers and analytics translators, supports the Proximus group in designing, developing and deploying internal AI-driven applications in line with the business needs.

As a Data Scientist, you will play a key role in developing end-to-end solutions for AI use cases. By taking up a use case right from the start you will help our customers define and scope the problem, pick the right ML solution and technology as well as ensure implementation, integration and deployment of your solution to production. You will do so while ensuring that our internal governance processes, technical excellence and best practices are respected. You will also collaborate with other team members in knowledge sharing as well as continuously improving our way of working.

Responsibilities


Develop AI applications using state-of-the-art (Gen)AI technology.
Translate business requests into AI applications.
Pilot prototypes in production processes to demonstrate their value.
Deploy prototypes to production to obtain reliable, scalable systems.
Present your results in a clear manner and discuss them with multi-functional project teams.
Work in close collaboration with business experts (e.g. for requirement gathering, data source identification, data and process understanding, feature engineering, result validation, etc.), and with other AI engineers in the team (e.g.forknowledgesharing).


Profile


PhD or Master's degree in Artificial Intelligence, Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field.
Proven experience (2+ years) in AI/ML engineering with a strong focus on deploying and integrating AI


Technical Skills


Strong expertise in Python
Strong knowledge of state-of-the-art AI and statistical methods
Proven proficiency with deploying scalable AI applications to production
Experience using LLM’s and commonly used libraries to interact with LLM’s (Langchain, Llamaindex, ...)
Hands-on experience with Azure Cloud, and Azure Data & AI services
Strong Expertise with version control (Git, GitHub)
Expertise with CI/CD pipelines, and modern software development best practices
Experience with Agentic AI frameworks (Langgraph, AutoGen, CrewAI, …) is a big plus
Experience with MLOps tools and frameworks (g. MLflow, Kubeflow, TFX) is a big plus


Attitudes/Behavior


Excellent problem-solving skills.
Collaboration: Strong interpersonal skills to work effectively with diverse stakeholders from data science, engineering, and business units.
Excellence: Strong commitment to excellence by consistently striving for high-quality outcomes, embracing continuous improvement, and maintaining a proactive, detail-oriented approach in all tasks.
Innovation Mindset: Willingness to explore new technologies and methodologies to drive continuous improvement.
Resilience: Capacity to troubleshoot and resolve issues in complex changing environments while maintaining a focus on quality and user experience.
Communication: Ability to convey complex technical concepts to non-technical audiences and provide clear documentation.


Languages


Fluent in English + French and/or Dutch

Title: Software Engineer
Description:
Senior C++ Developer - Broadcast Playout & Real-Time Media Systems

Location: Remote (UK / EU)

Type: Contract (rolling / indefinite)

Start Date: June 2026




We’re looking for a Senior Software Developer to join a globally recognised broadcast technology company powering thousands of TV channels across more than 50 countries worldwide.




This is a critical hire within the core Development team, focused primarily on a flagship, mission-critical broadcast playout platform used by major broadcasters globally.




You’ll play a key role in designing and evolving complex real-time broadcast software, working across modern C++, video/audio processing, graphics pipelines, and mission-critical playout systems.




What you’ll do

Design, develop, and improve features across a flagship broadcast playout platform
Work on real-time video and audio processing within mission-critical broadcast environments
Investigate and resolve software issues using crash dumps, logs, and debugging tools
Contribute to modernising and improving a long-standing C++ codebase
Develop and maintain unit tests and software quality processes
Configure and maintain development, testing, and build environments
Collaborate closely with support, delivery, and product teams to solve complex customer challenges
Mentor and support junior developers within the team
Contribute to coding standards, architecture discussions, and engineering best practices
Take ownership across the full software development lifecycle, from requirements through to customer deployment




Technical environment

Modern C++ (C++11, C++17)
Qt Framework
Real-time broadcast video and audio processing
Signal-level broadcast technologies and graphics pipelines
Broadcast playout and channel automation systems
Crash analysis, debugging, and fault investigation
Client/server communication and distributed systems
SQL, XML, and network synchronisation
Build systems and software tooling
Exposure to Matrox SDKs/hardware beneficial




What we’re looking for

Strong experience as a Senior Software Developer working with modern C++
Hands-on experience with real-time video and/or audio processing systems
Background within broadcast, media technology, playout, or signal-processing environments
Commercial experience using the Qt framework
Strong debugging and crash analysis capabilities within complex software systems
Experience delivering software products through the full SDLC
Ability to work effectively within a fully remote engineering team
Strong analytical problem-solving and communication skills
Experience mentoring developers or providing technical leadership is highly desirable
Experience working with broadcast vendors, playout providers, or production technology companies
Knowledge of graphics processing, video pipelines, or hardware SDK integrations




If this sounds like you, please do get in touch!

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Don’t settle for just any consulting company, choose the one that’s right for you!




At Data Wizards, we value perspeverance, curiosity, creativity, integrity and excellence. In that sense, we are looking for passionate AI & data professionals who want to help companies navigate the transition the world is facing today, and who want to be part of the Wizard adventure we are building.




On our side, we do our best to find exciting, challenging and meaningful projects for you. Today, we work with companies of all sizes, across various sectors, where we believe our work brings real value and purpose.




We are continuously on the lookout for bright data engineers, data scientists, machine learning engineers, AI engineers, and data analysts. But this job description is for Data Scientists out there feeling the magic in them and ready to spread it🪄




As a Data Scientist, your mission would include

Leading the technical aspects of data science projects, from conception to deployment.
Designing and implementing scalable data science solutions that integrate seamlessly with existing infrastructures.
Developing and optimizing machine learning models to drive actionable insights and automation.
Collaborating with cross-functional teams, including data engineers and business stakeholders, to align technical solutions with strategic goals.
Taking ownership of projects, ensuring high-quality deliverables in an agile environment.
Providing technical leadership and mentorship to junior data scientists.
Engaging in proof-of-concept (PoC) projects and scaling them into production-ready solutions.
Working in cloud-based environments and following MLOps best practices.
Communicating insights and technical solutions effectively to diverse audiences.




You’re a step closer to joining our team if you bring:




Background

Master’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, AI, or a related technical field.
5+ years of experience in Data Science, Machine Learning, or AI-driven projects.
Experience in diverse industries and solutions, demonstrating adaptability and problem-solving skills is considered as a plus.

Technical Skills

Strong programming skills in Python and SQL.
Expertise in machine learning frameworks (e.g., PyTorch, TensorFlow, Scikit-learn).
Hands-on experience with big data technologies and cloud platforms (AWS preferred; Azure is a plus).
Experience deploying ML models and following MLOps best practices.
Understanding of data engineering principles to support scalable AI solutions.
Ability to design and implement proof-of-concept (PoC) projects and scale them into production.

Soft Skills

Title: AI OPS Engineer (✨ immediate start ✨)
Description:
This is a Senior Platform Engineering role with an AI infrastructure focus — not an ML or data science role.







About Us




We are a venture builder creating purpose-driven, evidence-led startups.




We work hands-on with ventures from early validation through scale-up, providing shared infrastructure, operational excellence, and execution discipline — while keeping ventures autonomous and founder-led.




Our model is decentralized and pragmatic:




flat hierarchies
strong ownership
decisions grounded in data







The venture builder exists to accelerate execution, not to centralize power or slow teams down.




Most companies are running AI like it's 2023 — ad hoc tools, no cost attribution, no safety guarantees. We're building the infrastructure layer that changes that.

As our AI Ops Engineer, you'll own the platform that makes AI productive, safe, and economically rational across every team — engineering, product, and operations. This isn't tool configuration. It's building the operating layer that determines how fast our company can learn, ship, and compound.




Why this role exists




AI-assisted development has crossed the threshold from experiment to production system. But most companies are still running AI like it's 2023: ad hoc tool adoption, no cost attribution, no safety guarantees, no cross-team consistency. That's not a tooling gap — it's an infrastructure gap.

The AI Ops Engineer closes it.

You'll own the platform that makes AI productive, safe, and economically rational across every team — engineering, product, operations, and beyond. You're not configuring tools. You're building the operating layer that determines how fast this company can learn, ship, and compound.

If a Context Engineer makes AI effective within a codebase, the AI Ops Engineer makes AI effective everywhere — across repos, teams, workflows, and business functions.

This is one of the highest-leverage roles in the company.




What you'll own




AI-native CI/CD with quality, safety, and intelligence gates
Cross-repo agent infrastructure (Claude Code, MCP servers, shared context libraries)
Multi-model routing strategy and provider management
Token spend visibility and cost-per-outcome optimization
Internal automation workflows (n8n and beyond)
Security scanning and AI-specific risk management
Safe AI enablement for non-technical teams







You're likely a fit if




You automate your own workflows unprompted, have strong opinions about pipeline architecture, and get genuine satisfaction from systems that quietly work at scale.







Core background




DevOps, Platform Engineering, or Developer Experience — with deep CI/CD fluency, strong scripting ability, and an observability mindset. LLM API experience and familiarity with agent tooling are a strong plus.




This role reports to the VP of Engineering / CTO and has direct ownership of AI tooling spend. Compensation is benchmarked against senior platform engineering roles.

We're not hiring someone to keep the lights on. We're hiring someone to build the power plant.







Compensation philosophy




This role sits at the intersection of infrastructure, AI, and company-wide leverage. Compensation reflects that — it's benchmarked against senior platform engineering roles, not junior DevOps.







The mindset we're hiring for




This is not "keep the lights on." This is build the power plant — then make it reliable, safe, observable, scalable, and intelligent.




You'll be trusted with systems that influence how code gets written, reviewed, and shipped. How risk is controlled. How costs are managed. How quickly the entire company can learn, adapt, and execute.




Every team's ceiling is partly determined by the platform you build. Every dollar of AI spend either compounds or evaporates based on the infrastructure you design.




If that sounds exciting — if you want to build the platform that makes everyone else sharper, faster, and more capable — this role is for you.







Ideawise is an equal opportunity employer.

Title: AI+Data Engineer
Description:
🚀 Join Us as an AI Data Engineer at Biztory

At Biztory, we're not just building dashboards anymore. We're building autonomous, intelligent systems. From integrating LLMs into data pipelines to launching agentic services that think and act — we're shaping the future of AI-enabled data infrastructure. If that excites you, we want to hear from you.

🎯 What You'll Be Doing


Design and implement scalable, secure AI-powered data workflows
Build, fine-tune, and deploy autonomous agents that interact with data and systems (from service agents to sales reps)
Collaborate with our consulting teams to turn real client use cases into AI-powered solutions
Work with leading tech like Snowflake, Salesforce, OpenAI, Streamlit, and more
Be part of a growing team shaping the future of AI + Data Services in Europe


Requirements

🔍 Who We're Looking For

We want builders, not just coders. Whether you're self-taught or classically trained, junior or seasoned — if you live and breathe AI, data, and automation, you're our kind of engineer.

We're especially looking for:


Engineers who love working with Large Language Models, prompt engineering, and RAG architectures
People who are exploring agentic workflows, LLMOps, LangChain, OpenAI APIs, Vector DBs, and Snowflake Cortex
Tinkerers who see Autonomous Agents not as hype, but as the next productivity revolution
Proficiency in SQL and strong programming skills in Python (or equivalent (Java, JavaScript, C#, etc...)
Bonus if you have experience with AgentForce (Salesforce's framework for building autonomous agents)


Benefits

If you're still reading, you're probably one of us.

🎁 What We Offer


💰 Competitive salary, development incentives, and internal growth paths
🚗 Company car & fuel card
💻 Laptop and home office support
📚 Annual AI/tech talent plan to keep you ahead of the curve
🌍 Work on real projects, with real clients, pushing the boundaries of AI and data
🧠 Collaborate with a forward-thinking team that's building services like AgentForce from the ground up


📨 Sound like your kind of mission?

Send your CV or portfolio to careers@biztory.com.

Title: Senior Data Scientist - ML Engineer
Description:
About Devoteam:

Devoteam is a leading consulting firm focused on digital transformation. We help our clients leverage technology to achieve their business goals.

Our longstanding partnerships with leaders like AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft, and ServiceNow amplify our capabilities, allowing us to deliver top-tier expertise and award-winning services. We complement these partnerships with in-depth Cyber, Data & AI, Software development and multi cloud expertise.

For 30 years, we have always been guiding our customers on the next tech wave. Devoteam has guided organizations through the Internet and cloud. The next revolution? AI.

With 11,000 tech natives working in 25+ countries, we bring you highly skilled IT experts across Europe

At Devoteam, tech is in our DNA, and it’s the people we empower with tech. Now, with responsible AI, we’re ready to unlock a future where technology drives positive change.

Job Description

About the Role:

We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Senior Data Scientist - ML Engineer to join our growing Data & AI team (50 FTE) in Belgium. In this role, you will play a key part in designing, developing, and deploying machine learning solutions for our clients across various industries. You will collaborate with other data scientists, engineers, and consultants to deliver innovative and impactful solutions that address our clients' business challenges.

Responsibilities:


Design, develop, and deploy machine learning models and algorithms
Analyze large and complex datasets to identify patterns and insights
Build and maintain data pipelines for data ingestion, processing, and transformation
Collaborate with stakeholders to understand business requirements and translate them into technical solutions
Stay up to date with the latest advancements in machine learning and artificial intelligence
Contribute to the development of Devoteam's data science and machine learning capabilities



Qualifications

Qualifications:


Master's degree or Ph.D. in Computer Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or a related field
5+ years of experience in a data science or machine learning role
Strong programming skills in Python or R
Experience with machine learning frameworks such as TensorFlow, PyTorch, or scikit-learn
Experience with cloud platforms such as AWS, Azure, or GCP
Experience with Databricks is a plus
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills
Fluent in English and proficiency in Dutch or French is mandatory.
Sponsorship for a work permit not possible



Bonus Points:


Experience with big data technologies such as Hadoop, Spark, or Hive
Experience with MLOps tools and practices
Experience with natural language processing or computer vision
Contributions to open-source projects or publications in relevant conferences or journals



Additional Information

Why choose us?


Thrive in a fast-growing, entrepreneurial environment
Work with innovative and impactful Cloud, Data & AI projects for industry-leading clients
Connect with extraordinary talents in a collaborative and diverse culture
Enjoy a healthy work-life balance in an inspiring, eco-friendly workplace
Competitive compensation package



At Devoteam, we combine strong values – respect, frankness, ambition, entrepreneurship & collaboration – with a fun and supportive environment that empowers you to innovate, grow, and succeed in the fast-paced world of Cloud, Data & AI.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
🤖 The Job

At Dataroots, we couple our excellent consulting services with our technical expertise to build advanced machine learning models to improve business processes across a variety of industries, with a strong focus on DataOps and MLOps. As a Machine Learning Engineer, you'll be an integral part of our in-house team of AI specialists, designing and implementing models that deliver robust, data-driven solutions for our clients.




Passionate about data, you enjoy diving deep to uncover actionable insights and develop machine learning models that enhance client operations. You’ll experiment with different approaches, while collaborating with Data Engineers, Cloud Architects, and Data Strategists to drive the delivery of tailored, high-quality, end-to-end solutions.




You’ll also have the opportunity to mentor colleagues, share knowledge, and stay on top of industry developments through a range of initiatives we support internally.




🎉 What you'll find at Dataroots

Since 2016, Dataroots has been committing its excellent consulting services to help clients excel in their data-driven operations. Our approach focuses on team well-being, fostering diversity, and promoting knowledge sharing. By focusing on these values, we've achieved healthy and sustainable growth over the years. Through our consulting services, we actively encourage professional growth by exposing our employees to different industries, tools, technologies,... broadening their scope in the process.

As part of the Talan Group, we are able to take this approach to the next level, adding the element of international mobility to our employees’ career journey.

Join a team where your work will have a direct impact on both our clients and our journey. Learn more about our DNA at [https://dataroots.io/our-dna].




💼 What we’re looking for

You bring the following qualities & experiences to the role:

A deep passion for Machine Learning and a Master’s or PhD in a quantitative discipline.
Strong foundation in software design principles.
A minimum of 2 years of experience in machine learning and big data.
Expertise in machine learning and deep learning, with both a theoretical and practical grasp.
Proficiency in Python & SQL is a must; knowledge of R is an advantage.
Experience with deploying machine learning models and familiarity with MLOps practices.
At least a first experience with AWS, Azure or GCP and a solid understanding of cloud engineering and its use in Machine Learning.
Experience with ML versioning tooling.
Enthusiasm for working with large datasets and a pragmatic approach to problem-solving.
Strong communication skills and the ability to simplify complex technical concepts for clients.
Fluency in English and Dutch.




Don't worry if you don’t meet every requirement from the start. Most of all we are looking for colleagues with the same passion for personal and team development as the rest of us.




💰 The Offer

An attractive salary with extralegal benefits, including:

+ Mobility budget or a company car with fuel/charging card

+ Hospitalisation and group insurance

+ High-end laptop

+ Smartphone with subscription

+ Substantial amount of holidays

+ Meal vouchers

+ ...

Diverse and welcoming work environment, where you’ll collaborate & unwind with colleagues from different cultures and disciplines. The organization of both fun & professional events and initiatives is actively encouraged and supported.
A training budget for individual and team learning opportunities.
Tons of team-building events and sports initiatives to stay connected and unwind.




📍 Where you’ll work

Our main offices are in Leuven and Ghent, with co-working spaces in Charleroi and Antwerp. These spaces are perfect for both focusing and unwinding with colleagues, as well as brainstorming sessions and team events. While much of your work can be performed remotely, you will also work on-site with clients.




🎯 Ready to join Dataroots?

Excited to shape the future of AI as a Machine Learning Engineer with us? We’re looking forward to hearing from you! Apply now and start a new chapter with Dataroots! 🎉

Join a team where your work will have a direct impact on both our clients and our journey. Learn more about our DNA at [https://dataroots.io/our-dna].


💼 What we’re looking for

You bring the following qualities & experiences to the role:

  • A deep passion for Machine Learning and a Master’s or PhD in a quantitative discipline.
  • Strong foundation in software design principles.
  • A minimum of 2 years of experience in machine learning and big data.
  • Expertise in machine learning and deep learning, with both a theoretical and practical grasp.
  • Proficiency in Python & SQL is a must; knowledge of R is an advantage.
  • Experience with deploying machine learning models and familiarity with MLOps practices.
  • At least a first experience with AWS, Azure or GCP and a solid understanding of cloud engineering and its use in Machine Learning.
  • Experience with ML versioning tooling.
  • Enthusiasm for working with large datasets and a pragmatic approach to problem-solving.
  • Strong communication skills and the ability to simplify complex technical concepts for clients.
  • Fluency in English and Dutch.


Don't worry if you don’t meet every requirement from the start. Most of all we are looking for colleagues with the same passion for personal and team development as the rest of us.


💰 The Offer

  • An attractive salary with extralegal benefits, including:

+ Mobility budget or a company car with fuel/charging card

+ Hospitalisation and group insurance

+ High-end laptop

+ Smartphone with subscription

+ Substantial amount of holidays

+ Meal vouchers

+ ...

  • Diverse and welcoming work environment, where you’ll collaborate & unwind with colleagues from different cultures and disciplines. The organization of both fun & professional events and initiatives is actively encouraged and supported.
  • A training budget for individual and team learning opportunities.
  • Tons of team-building events and sports initiatives to stay connected and unwind.


📍 Where you’ll work

Our main offices are in Leuven and Ghent, with co-working spaces in Charleroi and Antwerp. These spaces are perfect for both focusing and unwinding with colleagues, as well as brainstorming sessions and team events. While much of your work can be performed remotely, you will also work on-site with clients.


🎯 Ready to join Dataroots?

Excited to shape the future of AI as a Machine Learning Engineer with us? We’re looking forward to hearing from you! Apply now and start a new chapter with Dataroots! 🎉

Title: Data Scientist Engineer
Description:
Devoteam is a leading consulting firm focused on digital transformation. We help our clients leverage technology to achieve their business goals.

Our longstanding partnerships with leaders like AWS, Google Cloud, Microsoft, and ServiceNow amplify our capabilities, allowing us to deliver top-tier expertise and award-winning services. We complement these partnerships with in-depth Cyber, Data & AI, Software development and multi cloud expertise.

For 30 years, we have always been guiding our customers on the next tech wave. Devoteam has guided organizations through the Internet and cloud. The next revolution? AI.

With 11,000 tech natives working in 25+ countries, we bring you highly skilled IT experts across Europe

At Devoteam, tech is in our DNA, and it’s the people we empower with tech. Now, with responsible AI, we’re ready to unlock a future where technology drives positive change.

Job Description

Are you passionate about data-driven platforms, machine learning models, and innovative applications? As a Data Scientist/Engineer at Devoteam, you'll play a hands-on role in delivering impactful solutions across various clients and industries. Each mission we undertake has clear deliverables and deadlines, ensuring you gain diverse experience. For example, one month you might develop a predictive maintenance model, and the next, a customer segmentation model for a retail client.

Key Responsibilities:


Platform Setup: Design and implement robust data platforms using Azure, AWS or Google Cloud and Databricks.
Data Management: Gather, prepare, and manage data for analysis.
Model Research: Investigate and select appropriate machine learning models.
ML Modeling: Develop and refine machine learning models.
Client Collaboration: Spend approximately 50% of your time working directly with clients, either independently or as part of a team.



Your Role:


Build and maintain data platforms and pipelines using Azure, AWS or Google Cloud, Databricks, and Python.
Develop predictive and machine learning models tailored to client needs.
Advise clients on translating their business requirements into technical solutions using machine learning techniques.
Stay updated on the latest trends in machine learning and AI, sharing insights with clients.
Act as a strategic partner to client Data Science teams, helping them navigate their challenges and aspirations.
Inspire clients with new data-driven opportunities.
Bring best practices in Git, DevOps, and CICD to enhance client data platforms.
Collaborate with Data Scientists, Data Engineers, and Business Stakeholders.
Represent Devoteam both online and offline, showcasing our expertise.



Qualifications

About You:


Reside in Belgium. (Mandatory)
Hold a university degree in Computer Science, (Business) Engineering, or a specialized master's in Machine Learning or AI.
Minimum 3 years experience in a similar role.
Have a solid understanding of various machine learning models (e.g., SVM, RNN, Deep Learning) and their applications.
Possess foundational knowledge of Data Science, including sampling techniques and evaluation metrics.
Proficient in SQL and Python.
Experience with Spark and/or cloud ecosystems is a must.
Experience with Databricks is real asset
Eager to learn and passionate about data and machine learning technologies.
Excited to join a dynamic and growing team.
Fluent in English; proficiency in Dutch or French is mandatory
Sponsorship for a work permit not possible



Additional Information

Why choose us?


Thrive in a fast-growing, entrepreneurial environment
Work with innovative and impactful Cloud, Data & AI projects for industry-leading clients
Connect with extraordinary talents in a collaborative and diverse culture
Enjoy a healthy work-life balance in an inspiring, eco-friendly workplace
Competitive compensation package



At Devoteam, we combine strong values – respect, frankness, ambition, entrepreneurship & collaboration – with a fun and supportive environment that empowers you to innovate, grow, and succeed in the fast-paced world of Cloud, Data & AI.

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Description:
Senior Data Scientist (Python / ML / SQL / Cloud)

Remote (EU-based contractors)

€500/day

Immediate Start




We’re working with a globally recognised digital & technology-led organisation undergoing a major data transformation programme across its European operations.




This is not a pure research role.




You’ll be joining a high-performing data function where the focus is on building and deploying real-world decision models that directly impact commercial performance across digital and eCommerce channels.




The Project

This programme is centred around unlocking value from large-scale digital, retail, and media datasets, including complex third-party and privacy-safe data environments.

You’ll be responsible for taking new and existing data sources and turning them into:

Scalable decision models
Actionable insights for commercial teams
Logic that powers internal tools and workflows

The work sits at the intersection of data science, product, and commercial strategy — with a strong emphasis on impact and adoption, not just experimentation.




What You’ll Be Doing

Researching and onboarding new data sources (APIs, third-party datasets, clean-room environments)
Translating business problems into clear modelling and analytical approaches
Building decision models, forecasting frameworks, and recommendation logic
Applying ML/AI techniques where appropriate, with a focus on explainability and measurable outcomes
Working closely with BI and data engineering teams to productionise models
Supporting client-facing discussions, explaining methodology and driving adoption
Contributing to commercial work, including proposals and case studies




What They’re Looking For

8+ years in Data Science / Applied ML / Advanced Analytics
Strong Python for modelling and analysis (production-quality code)
Advanced SQL and experience working with large, complex datasets
Solid grounding in statistics, experimentation, and measurement
Experience building models that are actually deployed and used, not just prototypes
MLOps exposure (deployment, monitoring, retraining strategies)
Comfortable working cross-functionally with data engineering and BI teams
Strong communication, able to explain complex ideas clearly to non-technical stakeholders

Nice to have:

Experience in digital, eCommerce, retail media, or marketplace environments
Exposure to privacy-safe / clean-room data environments
Cloud data stack experience
NLP / GenAI applied to commercial use cases




Important

EU-based contractors only
Must have a registered company set up and ready to freelance
Immediate availability required




If you’re looking for a role where your models actually drive decisions, not just sit in notebooks, this is a strong one to get involved in.

Title: AI Specialist
Description:
About Us

We are a well-established Belgian company in the insurance sector. Our flagship product is a SaaS platform used by insurance brokers, backed by over 20 years of success and a highly efficient, robust codebase built primarily in .NET.

We are currently in a strategic phase of innovation, integrating Artificial Intelligence to revolutionize how our users interact with our platform. To achieve this, we are building an ecosystem of Python microservices that communicate with our core system via APIs, and we are looking to expand our AI team to accelerate this journey.

The Role

You will join a growing Artificial Intelligence team, working closely with our AI Specialist. Your primary mission will be to design, develop, and deploy Generative AI solutions that deliver direct value to our users and optimize internal processes.

This is not just an R&D or experimental role; we have an ambitious roadmap to launch these new AI features into production. Our SaaS is used daily by thousands of professionals as their primary work tool. This means you will tackle real-world challenges related to high volume, scale, and performance, taking your code from development straight to the end-user.

You will have end-to-end ownership of your development lifecycle, supported by our Infrastructure and DevOps team.

What you will do in your day-to-day

Develop and implement AI-driven features, such as intelligent email analysis, automated data extraction from documents, conversational assistants, and complex RAG systems.
Build and maintain efficient, scalable microservices in Python using FastAPI.
Apply Prompt Engineering techniques, build RAG architectures, and integrate LLMs using frameworks like LangChain and LangGraph.
Design and implement robust LLM evaluation pipelines to ensure the quality, accuracy, and reliability of our generative models in production, leveraging observability tools like LangFuse.
Collaborate closely with the .NET development team to ensure seamless integration of AI services via APIs.
Engage with technical and business stakeholders to understand requirements and translate user needs into technical solutions.
Explore and evaluate new AI technologies, with the potential to dive into model fine-tuning and traditional Machine Learning in the future.
Manage the deployment and monitoring of your services in Azure, taking end-to-end responsibility with the backing of our infrastructure team.

What we are looking for

Demonstrable Mid-level experience in software engineering, with a strong focus on Python and API development (FastAPI).
Hands-on experience working with Large Language Models (LLMs), RAG architectures, and Prompt Engineering.
Familiarity with the Generative AI ecosystem and tooling; specifically, practical knowledge of frameworks like LangChain/LangGraph and monitoring/evaluation tools like LangFuse.
Experience working with cloud providers, preferably Azure (and familiarity with tools like Azure AI Foundry).
A strong "get things done" and product-oriented mentality. You are driven by shipping features to production, adding tangible value to customers, gathering real-world feedback, and iteratively improving the product.
An inquisitive, adaptable mindset with a strong capacity to learn and stay continuously up-to-date with the latest trends, tools, and breakthroughs in the rapidly evolving AI landscape.
Excellent communication skills to interact effectively with various stakeholders across the company.

Nice-to-haves

Knowledge or familiarity with .NET/C# to better understand the core of our platform.
Previous experience with fine-tuning open-source models or traditional Machine Learning.
Familiarity with LLM evaluation methodologies.

Title: Fullstack Software Engineer (x/f/m) - Global Billing Platform
Description:
Health can’t wait.

Not for symptoms to get worse. Not for a six‑month appointment. Not for a system to catch up. But that’s exactly how healthcare works today. You wait, until you can’t.

Alan exists to end the wait.

Health is a universal right, and we believe this right can only become real when it’s coupled with prevention. We need to stop treating health as something we repair and start treating it as something we build, every day. It’s not solely a question of willpower. It’s the healthcare system itself that needs to work for everyone, in a sustainable way.

So we are building the new standard in prevention insurance. Alan is the first company that integrates insurance, prevention, and care into a single, acclaimed user experience.

We are on an incredible journey to build a global leading company, with a unique culture. We already partner with 40K+ companies of all sizes, serving more than 1M+ members, and have reached €800M+ in ARR.

Prevention as the new norm. That's what we're building with our team of 800+ people. If it speaks to you: we're hiring across France, Spain, Belgium, and Canada. And beyond.

⭐ Build the backbone of Alan’s financial systems

At Alan, billing is a platform that powers reliable, accurate, and scalable financial operations across multiple countries and products. Our mission is to turn complex, high-volume billing flows into systems that “just work,” so customers and members can focus on what matters.

As a Software Engineer on this team, you will:


Build the core billing engine: process thousands of transactions daily with precision and reliability.
Design modular, reusable billing primitives and abstractions that scale across products and regions.
Integrate seamlessly with internal systems and external payment platforms.


Automate workflows and billing schedules at scale for companies of all sizes.


Ensure invoices are accurate, reliable, and easy to manage for internal and external users.
Collaborate closely with product, design, and operations to maintain a unified, high-quality platform experience.


Why this role is special:


Solve technically challenging problems in a mission-critical, high-scale platform.
Shape the architecture of Alan’s core financial systems and long-term product strategy.
Impact millions of members and clients, powering hassle-free experience.
Join a team obsessed with craftsmanship, scalability, and operational excellence.
Work in an environment where your ideas, designs, and decisions drive tangible outcomes.


🛠 Your playground


Stack: Python/Flask, React, React Native, PostgreSQL.
What you’ll do: ship scalable, reliable systems and build the backbone of Alan’s billing operations.
Impact: your work directly unlocks new services, countries, and exciting deals. It then supports their reliable operation.


👥 Who we’re looking for


We are looking for software engineers of all experience levels, ideally with backend / full-stack experience.
Passionate about navigating complex business logic and system dependencies.
Experienced with scalable, resilient platform design.
Well-versed in OOP languages (Python, Java, C#).
Fluent in English (French is a plus but not required).
Curious, self-starting, humble, collaborative, and eager to grow.


🙌 Perks & Benefits


Fair rewards & equity packages.
Flexible office & remote options.
Top-tier equipment: MacBook Pro, monitor, noise-canceling headphones.
Flexible vacation & hours.
Comprehensive healthcare for you, children, and partners.
Transport support and coaching for personal growth.
Training budget for conferences, courses, books.
Extended parental leave for all parents.


🔄 Recruitment note

🌟 We hire people, not roles. Don’t worry if you don’t check every box - high potential and curiosity matter most. We may even suggest another team if we see a better fit.

🔖 Learn more About Alan and on our Career pages, Medium, blog and Glassdoor page.

Why this role is special:

  • Solve technically challenging problems in a mission-critical, high-scale platform.
  • Shape the architecture of Alan’s core financial systems and long-term product strategy.
  • Impact millions of members and clients, powering hassle-free experience.
  • Join a team obsessed with craftsmanship, scalability, and operational excellence.
  • Work in an environment where your ideas, designs, and decisions drive tangible outcomes.

🛠 Your playground

  • Stack: Python/Flask, React, React Native, PostgreSQL.
  • What you’ll do: ship scalable, reliable systems and build the backbone of Alan’s billing operations.
  • Impact: your work directly unlocks new services, countries, and exciting deals. It then supports their reliable operation.

👥 Who we’re looking for

  • We are looking for software engineers of all experience levels, ideally with backend / full-stack experience.
  • Passionate about navigating complex business logic and system dependencies.
  • Experienced with scalable, resilient platform design.
  • Well-versed in OOP languages (Python, Java, C#).
  • Fluent in English (French is a plus but not required).
  • Curious, self-starting, humble, collaborative, and eager to grow.

🙌 Perks & Benefits

  • Fair rewards & equity packages.
  • Flexible office & remote options.
  • Top-tier equipment: MacBook Pro, monitor, noise-canceling headphones.
  • Flexible vacation & hours.
  • Comprehensive healthcare for you, children, and partners.
  • Transport support and coaching for personal growth.
  • Training budget for conferences, courses, books.
  • Extended parental leave for all parents.

🔄 Recruitment note

🌟 We hire people, not roles. Don’t worry if you don’t check every box - high potential and curiosity matter most. We may even suggest another team if we see a better fit.

🔖 Learn more About Alan and on our Career pages, Medium, blog and Glassdoor page.

Title: Machine Learning Researcher
Description:
We are looking for a experienced ML Researcher/ Engineer to maintain and expand the machine learning systems that drive our core business: real-time esports pricing. Our models directly dictate market odds for high-volume titles (CS2, Dota 2, LoL), and our product is 100% reliant on the accuracy and latency of these models. We are looking for a senior engineer who possesses both mathematical intuition and engineering rigor required to serve these models in highly volatile, live environments. To complement your core skills, you are able to leverage modern AI tooling to accelerate your workflow and rapidly prototype solutions.







Responsibilities

Design, train, and deploy probabilistic ML models that process real-time game data to estimate accurate live market odds.
Own the end-to-end model lifecycle; from driving the applied research and prototyping of custom architectures to productionizing them for reliable training and highly optimized, low-latency inference.
Collaborate directly with trading and product teams to formulate current challenges, translate raw model outputs into actionable insights, and serve as the technical authority on model performance and pricing accuracy.
Tech Stack: Python · PyTorch · scikit-learn · ONNX Runtime · Streamlit · AWS (S3, ECR, Lambda, SageMaker) · Docker · MLflow · SkyPilot







Required Skills & Experience

Mindset: A problem-solving mindset, strong analytical skills, and a passion for building impactful ML solutions.
ML Depth & Pragmatism: Deep understanding of machine learning fundamentals, deep learning, and statistical modeling. You have the mathematical foundation to build custom architectures, combined with the intuition of knowing which approach to pick and why.
Rigorous Validation: Practical experience with rigorous model validation in high-stakes production environments. You don’t just check MSE; you stress-test models against edge cases and volatile live game states.
Autonomy & Ownership: Demonstrated ability to take ambiguous business problems, define the technical ML framing, and drive the project independently from conception to deployment.
AI-Augmented Engineering: Strong command of modern AI tooling to accelerate coding workflows, assist in custom model design, and rapidly prototype solutions without sacrificing code quality or system rigor.




Bonus Qualifications

Esports Domain Knowledge: You understand the mechanics, meta, and competitive landscapes of titles like CS2, Dota 2 or LoL.
Betting & Quantitative Finance: Prior experience building pricing models for sports betting, algorithmic trading, or high-frequency trading environments.

.

Haven't we caught you yet?

Help us maintain our position as the No. 1 esports analytics company
Imagine a culture that embraces change and quickly adapts to global opportunities.
Experience immense potential for personal growth while contributing to an innovative team.
Work remotely or in a hybrid style – whatever suits you best.
Enjoy our brand new Norse-myth-inspired offices in Prague.
Improve your English with company-sponsored language courses.
Participate in quarterly team-building activities to bond with your team.
Benefit from a sponsored Multisport card.

Title: Software Developer (React/C#)
Description:
Onderneming




Deze start-up software onderneming te Kampenhout voorziet in een volledige implementatie van IT producten bij zowel kleine als grote ondernemingen. Ze maken het verschil door volledige en gedetailleerde analyses te maken betreffende verschillende ERP mogelijkheden om zo af te kunnen stemmen wat de meest optimale oplossing is.




Takenpakket




Als Software Developer werk je projectmatig aan de ontwikkeling van nieuwe applicaties, met een sterke focus op frontend development in React. Je analyseert de functionele en technische behoeften en vertaalt deze naar performante, gebruiksvriendelijke en schaalbare oplossingen.




Naast je focus op React krijg je de mogelijkheid om jezelf verder te ontwikkelen richting fullstack development. Op termijn krijg je de kans om ook backendtechnologieën zoals C# en Python onder de knie te krijgen en mee te werken aan het volledige developmentproces van frontend tot backend. Zo bouw je stap voor stap expertise op binnen het volledige softwarelandschap en neem je ownership over projecten van analyse tot oplevering.




Profiel




Je beschikt over een masterdiploma binnen IT, Burgerlijk Ingenieur, Industrieel Ingenieur of een gelijkaardige richting, of je behaalde een bachelordiploma aangevuld met meerdere jaren relevante ervaring in development.
Je hebt een sterk analytisch inzicht en haalt energie uit het analyseren, optimaliseren en uitwerken van technische oplossingen.
Je communiceert vlot, werkt graag samen en kan tegelijkertijd zelfstandig verantwoordelijkheid opnemen binnen projecten.
Je hebt een uitgesproken interesse in softwareontwikkeling en bouwt graag zelf toepassingen uit, bij voorkeur met technologieën zoals C# of Python.




Aanbod




Brutoloon naar ervaring
Bedrijfswagen + tank/laadkaart of mobiliteitsbudget
Hospitalisatieverzekering
Netto onkostenvergoedingen
Maaltijdcheques
3 dagen thuiswerk
Veel flexibiliteit
Werken in een bedrijf met een vlakke in-house structuur

Title: AI Trainer
Description:
About the Role

We are seeking highly qualified Computer Science experts to review advanced Python-based Questions & Solutions (QSAs) developed for Master’s and PhD-level assessments. The ideal candidate will possess strong theoretical foundations, advanced problem-solving abilities, and the capability to evaluate solutions from both academic and systems perspectives.

The reviewer will be responsible for validating the correctness, depth, computational efficiency, scalability, and research-level rigor of coding solutions while ensuring alignment with high academic standards.

Key Responsibilities

Review and validate advanced Python coding questions and solutions.
Evaluate correctness, optimality, scalability, and completeness of solutions.
Verify complexity analysis, proof-style reasoning, and formal justifications.
Identify logical inconsistencies, hidden edge cases, and performance bottlenecks.
Assess code quality, readability, modularity, and architectural design.
Ensure solutions meet Master’s/PhD-level academic and technical standards.
Review pseudocode derived from research papers and validated implementation accuracy.
Provide clear, structured, and actionable feedback to content creators.

Required Technical Expertise

Core Computer Science Foundations

Strong understanding of time and space complexity analysis.
Knowledge of computational complexity theory including:
P, NP, NP-Complete, NP-Hard, PSPACE, EXPTIME
Familiarity with computability theory and formal correctness reasoning.

Advanced Algorithms & Data Structures

Dynamic Programming and advanced optimization techniques.
Graph algorithms, network flow, SCCs, and approximation algorithms.
Strong understanding of advanced data structures such as:
Segment Trees
Fenwick Trees
Tries
Suffix Arrays
Persistent Data Structures
Skip Lists

Software Engineering & Systems Knowledge

Strong debugging and edge-case evaluation skills.
Experience with unit testing and modular software design.
Understanding of performance trade-offs and optimization strategies.
Familiarity with concurrency, distributed systems, and systems-level reasoning.

Programming Paradigms

Object-Oriented Programming and SOLID principles.
Functional programming concepts such as immutability and higher-order functions.
Exposure to declarative or logic programming concepts is a plus.

Qualifications

Master’s or PhD in Computer Science or a related field preferred.
Strong expertise in Python programming.
Prior experience in research, academic review, coding assessments, or technical evaluation preferred.
Experience in competitive programming, research labs, or systems development is an advantage.
